THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the true isometric angle, what the 5-7 hero details are, and how to keep the composition balanced and uncluttered. THEN GENERATE: render the scene at a clean ~45-degree isometric (or 2:1 game-iso) angle as a self-contained cube/slice of world floating on soft background; fill it with neatly arranged miniature props that tell the story of the place, with crisp clean edges and a soft toy-like, low-poly-meets-detailed look; light it with soft global illumination and a gentle key for the [TIME OF DAY], adding subtle ambient occlusion in the corners; keep a soft contact shadow beneath the floating base. CONSTRAINTS: maintain a consistent isometric projection (no random perspective warp), keep scale believable for a miniature, tasteful detail not clutter, no text or logos, one cohesive scene only. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished isometric diorama only in a 1:1 frame, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 1:1 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the time of day or add a focal prop.
h: Describe the [SUBJECT] world to build (e.g. cozy coffee shop, hacker bedroom), set a [PALETTE], and optionally the [TIME OF DAY].

p: ROLE: You are a motion-design CGI artist rendering a high-end Cinema 4D + Octane abstract key visual. TASK: Render [SUBJECT] as a cinematic soft-body 3D composition in a [MATERIAL] finish with a dreamy [PALETTE] gradient. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the hero shape, the supporting soft forms that orbit it, and the gradient lighting direction that gives it that premium glow. THEN GENERATE: build the subject and a few inflated soft-body forms (puffed, rounded, gently squished like air-filled latex or pillows) floating in a smooth gradient environment; give surfaces the chosen [MATERIAL] with realistic soft reflections, gentle subsurface and clean highlights; light with large soft area lights and colored gradient bounce for that studio motion-design feel; add tasteful depth-of-field blur and a faint floor with soft reflections. CONSTRAINTS: keep it clean and intentional (no chaotic noise), physically believable soft materials, no text or logos, balanced composition with real negative space, family-safe. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished C4D-style render only in a 4:5 frame, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the palette or the soft material.
h: Name the [SUBJECT] or shape to feature, pick a [PALETTE] (e.g. peach-and-lilac gradient), and optionally a [MATERIAL] (e.g. inflated latex, frosted glass).

p: ROLE: You are a collectible toy designer rendering a designer vinyl figure for a product page. TASK: Turn [SUBJECT] into a stylized collectible vinyl figurine in a [POSE/THEME], standing on a [BASE]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide which 2-3 signature traits to keep so it's instantly recognizable, the head-to-body proportion, and the pose that reads from across a shelf. THEN GENERATE: model the subject as a chunky stylized vinyl toy with a slightly oversized head, simplified friendly features, smooth molded surfaces and a clean glossy painted finish with subtle seam lines like a real production figure; pose it on a tasteful display base; render against a soft neutral studio backdrop with even product lighting, a soft contact shadow and a gentle specular highlight on the vinyl. CONSTRAINTS: keep the subject's key identifying features and color story accurate while clearly stylized; one figure only, family-friendly, realistic toy materials (visible plastic, not skin pores), no added text or logos, no extra props beyond the base. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished collectible render only in a 4:5 frame, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the pose or the base.
h: Describe the [SUBJECT] to figurize, set the [POSE/THEME] (e.g. heroic stance, holding a coffee), and optionally a [BASE] style.

p: ROLE: You are a technical CGI artist rendering an exploded-view product diagram for a spec page. TASK: Render [SUBJECT] as a clean exploded view with its parts separated in order along a [AXIS] axis on a [BACKDROP]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): identify the logical layers/components of this object, the order they assemble in, and the spacing that keeps each part readable without overlap. THEN GENERATE: separate the object into its believable component layers and float them evenly apart along the chosen axis, each part perfectly aligned as if mid-assembly; render with crisp clean edges, accurate PBR materials and subtle thin alignment lines hinting how parts fit together; light it with even soft studio light, gentle ambient occlusion and soft contact shadows on the backdrop so depth reads clearly. CONSTRAINTS: keep each component faithful to the real object's design and materials; believable, physically sensible separation (no invented internals that don't make sense); clean and uncluttered, no callout text or numbers, one product only. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished exploded-view render only in a 4:5 frame, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the axis or the backdrop.
h: Name the [SUBJECT] to explode, set the [BACKDROP] (e.g. white seamless, blueprint blue), and optionally the [AXIS] direction (e.g. vertical, diagonal).

p: ROLE: You are an archviz artist rendering a photoreal interior in a Blender Cycles-style path-traced pipeline. TASK: Render [SUBJECT] as a believable interior scene with [LIGHT MOOD] lighting and a [MATERIAL PALETTE]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the camera height and lens for a natural eye-level view, the main daylight source, and how light bounces and pools across surfaces. THEN GENERATE: build a tasteful, uncluttered interior with realistic furniture and props at correct scale; drive the lighting with soft global illumination from a window plus subtle bounce, casting gentle directional shadows and warm/cool color from the [LIGHT MOOD]; give every surface accurate PBR materials (wood grain, fabric weave, matte walls, subtle floor reflections) with believable roughness and soft ambient occlusion in corners; keep a calm, balanced composition with natural negative space. CONSTRAINTS: realistic architectural proportions and straight verticals (no warped walls), no impossible floating furniture, believable physically based lighting (no flat AI glow), no text or logos, one cohesive room. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished interior render only in a 3:2 frame, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 3:2 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the light mood or the material palette.
h: Describe the [SUBJECT] room (e.g. minimalist living room, reading nook), set the [LIGHT MOOD] (e.g. golden hour, overcast soft), and optionally a [MATERIAL PALETTE].

p: ROLE: You are a miniature painter and 3D artist rendering a collector-grade tabletop figure. TASK: Render [SUBJECT] as a hand-painted [THEME] miniature on a [BASE], shot macro like a hobby showcase. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the heroic pose, the scale cues that sell it as a small figure, and where brush highlights should fall to read the form. THEN GENERATE: model the subject as a small detailed figurine with sculpted folds and gear, finished in believable hand-painted acrylics — matte base coats, subtle layering, edge highlights and gentle washes in the recesses; mount it on a textured scenic [BASE]; shoot it in tight macro with shallow depth of field, soft hobby-desk lighting and a softly blurred background so it reads as a real painted mini photographed up close. CONSTRAINTS: keep the paint finish matte-realistic (no glossy plastic or chrome), believable miniature scale and detail, family-safe, no text or logos, one figure only on its base. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished miniature render only in a 4:5 frame, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the theme or the base.
h: Describe the [SUBJECT] mini, set the [THEME] (e.g. fantasy knight, sci-fi scout), and optionally a [BASE] (e.g. mossy stone, desert sand).

p: ROLE: You are a 3D icon designer making a glossy app-store-grade icon. TASK: Render [SUBJECT] as a clean, tactile 3D icon in a [MATERIAL] finish with a [PALETTE] color scheme. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): simplify the concept to its single clearest shape, decide the soft rounding, and choose the light angle that gives it that friendly squishy highlight. THEN GENERATE: model the concept as a single rounded, simplified 3D object with smooth bevels and soft edges; give it the chosen [MATERIAL] with realistic glossy or frosted reflections, a gentle specular highlight and subtle subsurface glow; center it on a soft solid or gently graded background that complements the palette; light with soft studio lighting and add a soft drop shadow beneath so it floats cleanly. CONSTRAINTS: keep it simple, centered and instantly legible at small sizes; one icon only, balanced and symmetrical feel, no text or letters, no busy background, family-safe. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished 3D icon render only in a 1:1 frame, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 1:1 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the material or the palette.
h: Describe the [SUBJECT] icon concept (e.g. a rocket, a heart, a wallet), pick a [PALETTE], and optionally a [MATERIAL] (e.g. glossy plastic, frosted glass, metal).

p: ROLE: You are a stylized 3D artist rendering a low-poly game asset beauty shot. TASK: Render [SUBJECT] as a clean low-poly stylized asset in a [PALETTE] color scheme on a [BACKGROUND]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide how few faceted planes still read the silhouette, the flat color blocking, and the angle that shows the form best. THEN GENERATE: model the subject from deliberate low-poly faceted geometry with clean visible edges and simplified flat-shaded color regions; keep the look stylized and friendly, not photoreal; set it against a soft solid or gently graded [BACKGROUND]; light with soft ambient fill plus one gentle key so facets catch subtle shading, and add a soft contact shadow beneath to ground it like a turntable render. CONSTRAINTS: maintain a consistent low-poly style throughout (no mixing in high-detail photoreal bits), keep the silhouette readable, no text or logos, one asset only, clean and uncluttered. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished low-poly render only in a 1:1 frame, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 1:1 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the palette or the background.
h: Describe the [SUBJECT] asset (e.g. a tree, a campfire, a little robot), pick a [PALETTE], and optionally a [BACKGROUND] tone.

p: ROLE: You are a CGI artist specializing in hyper-reflective metal renders. TASK: Render [SUBJECT] as a flowing [METAL] form mid-morph, with mirror reflections inside a moody studio toned toward [ENVIRONMENT]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the direction of the liquid motion, the hero angle, and how the studio environment should map across the reflective surface to read as metal. THEN GENERATE: shape the subject from smooth, flowing metal that looks caught mid-motion with soft drips, ripples or stretched highlights; give it a flawless [METAL] surface with sharp, believable reflections of a simple gradient studio in the [ENVIRONMENT] tone; light with controlled studio softboxes and accent rims so the metal sparkles with intent; add a faint reflective floor and soft shadow to ground it. CONSTRAINTS: reflections must look physically plausible (mirror the environment, not random noise), keep the silhouette recognizable, photoreal metal (no flat gray paint look), no text or logos, one form only. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished metal render only in a 4:5 frame, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the metal type or the environment tone.
h: Name the [SUBJECT] or shape to render in metal, pick the [METAL] (e.g. liquid chrome, brushed gold, iridescent), and optionally an [ENVIRONMENT] tone for reflections.

p: ROLE: You are a CGI artist rendering an educational cross-section cutaway for a product or science visual. TASK: Render [SUBJECT] sliced cleanly to reveal its internal layers, emphasizing the [HIGHLIGHT], on a [BACKDROP]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide where to cut for the most informative reveal, what believable internal layers this object has, and how to keep the intact half recognizable. THEN GENERATE: cut the object with one clean flat plane so half shows the real exterior and half reveals plausible internal structure in cross-section; render the cut faces crisply with accurate materials and subtle layer separation, gently drawing the eye to the [HIGHLIGHT]; light with even soft studio light, soft ambient occlusion in the interior recesses and a gentle contact shadow on the [BACKDROP]; keep the whole thing clean, clear and three-dimensional. CONSTRAINTS: internals must be believable and consistent with the real object (no invented fantasy parts), keep the exterior faithful, no callout text or labels, photoreal PBR materials, one object only. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished cutaway render only in a 4:5 frame, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to move the cut or change which layer is highlighted.
h: Name the [SUBJECT] to cut away, set the [BACKDROP], and optionally the [HIGHLIGHT] layer to emphasize (e.g. the core, the filling).

p: ROLE: You are a 3D artist specializing in cozy handcrafted textile renders. TASK: Render [SUBJECT] as a warm handmade craft object built from soft felt and knitted yarn in a [PALETTE], using a [TEXTURE MIX]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide which parts read as smooth felt versus chunky knit, where visible stitching belongs, and how to keep it cute and recognizable. THEN GENERATE: model the subject from soft craft materials — fuzzy felt surfaces, chunky knitted ribbing, twisted woolly yarn and tiny visible stitches and seams that prove it's handmade; keep shapes rounded and huggable; place it in a simple matching soft-craft setting; light with warm, soft, gentle studio light and shallow depth of field so the fuzzy fibers catch the light and the background blurs softly. CONSTRAINTS: every surface reads as real textile (no plastic, glass or metal sheen), keep the silhouette charming and clear, family-safe, no text or logos, one cohesive subject. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished craft render only in a 1:1 frame, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 1:1 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the palette or the texture mix.
h: Describe the [SUBJECT] to craft (e.g. a fox, a coffee cup, a tiny house), pick a [PALETTE], and optionally the [TEXTURE MIX] (e.g. felt + knit, all wool).

p: You are a lead character designer at a Japanese animation studio who writes image prompts for GPT-4o / Gemini and Midjourney. Produce ONE clean anime CHARACTER REFERENCE SHEET (model sheet). Character: [CHARACTER]. Series vibe: [SERIES VIBE]. Palette: [PALETTE]. THINK FIRST (silently, never shown): the single silhouette that makes this character readable from across a room, the three or four 'design hooks' (a scar, an asymmetric sleeve, a signature accessory) that fans would recognize, and a tight 4-6 color palette that stays consistent across every view. Then write ONE copy-paste image description in flowing natural sentences covering, in order: a neutral standing A-pose shown in three orthographic views side by side on a plain off-white sheet (front, three-quarter, back), consistent height and proportions across all three, hairstyle and how it falls from each angle, face with anime-stylized eyes and neutral expression, costume with named fabrics and the design hooks called out, footwear, and the locked color palette swatches lined up along the bottom. Use an 85mm-portrait look, true-to-life fur texture, sharp eye catchlights, and crisp premium business-headshot polish. Mood: dignified, charming, subtly funny. Leave a clean band of negative space and place a brief high-contrast title reading '[TITLE]', a few words only, so it stays legible. Mood: homey, fresh, save-worthy. Begin with 'I NEED a styled flat-lay image with the title spelled correctly:'. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly, nothing else: 1) PROMPT: <one paragraph> 2) TEXT TIP: <one line reminding to keep on-image words to 2-5 for clean rendering>.
h: Name the [DISH OR TOPIC]; set the [MOOD/PALETTE] (e.g. cozy rustic); add a short [TITLE].

Light it dramatically with controlled studio or directional light, crisp focus on the hero element, and a cohesive [PALETTE/MOOD] that feels premium and intentional. Mood: smart, bold, memorable, billboard-worthy. Begin with 'I NEED a photorealistic conceptual ad image of:'. p: ROLE: You are a feature-animation art director writing one DALL·E 3 poster scene in flowing natural language (no flags or weights). p: You are a senior character concept artist at a AAA game studio who writes image prompts for GPT-4o / Gemini and Midjourney. Turn my idea into ONE cinematic, photoreal-painted character key art. Character: [CHARACTER]. World/era: [WORLD/ERA]. Mood: [MOOD]. p: You are a matte-painting landscape artist for fantasy films, writing prompts for GPT-4o / Gemini and Midjourney. Paint ONE breathtaking, vast fantasy vista. p: You are a hard-surface vehicle and mech concept designer who writes image prompts for GPT-4o / Gemini and Midjourney. Design ONE believable sci-fi machine. Machine type: [MACHINE TYPE]. p: You are a world-building art director who designs faction identities and writes image prompts for GPT-4o / Gemini and Midjourney. Design ONE coherent heraldic identity for an invented faction. Nothing else.

OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) PROMPT: <the full description + param line> 2) FX NOTE: <one line on how the spell light interacts with the scene> 3) ELEMENT SWAP: <one phrase that changes the element and recolors the light>. p: You are a cinematic concept artist staging boss encounters for games, writing prompts for GPT-4o / Gemini and Midjourney. Compose ONE dramatic hero-versus-boss confrontation. Hero: [HERO]. p: ROLE: You are a fashion-magazine cover photographer and beauty retoucher shooting the lead image for an issue. TASK: Render [SUBJECT] wearing [OUTFIT/LOOK] as a polished editorial beauty portrait with cover-ready composition. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the crop (tight head-and-shoulders vs. waist-up), the eyeline, and where to leave clean space at the top for a masthead and cover lines. THEN GENERATE: frame the subject head-on with a confident, magnetic gaze into the lens; light the face with a soft beauty dish slightly above and a subtle fill so skin reads luminous with real pores and fine texture (not airbrushed plastic); set them against a seamless [BACKDROP COLOR] backdrop; shoot at roughly 85mm with a wide aperture for a flattering shallow depth and creamy fall-off; carry the [MOOD] through expression and tonal grade; keep clean negative space above the head. CONSTRAINTS: realistic skin with visible pores and natural catchlights in the eyes, accurate five-finger hands if hands appear, no warped jewelry or melted fabric, no extra teeth, no added text or logos in the image, one subject only. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished cover-portrait image only in a 4:5 vertical, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw --v 6'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the backdrop color or tighten the crop.
h: Describe the [SUBJECT] (or attach a photo), name the [OUTFIT/LOOK], and optionally set the [MOOD] and [BACKDROP COLOR].

p: ROLE: You are a high-fashion studio photographer and art director producing a single hero frame for an editorial spread. TASK: Photograph [SUBJECT] in [OUTFIT] against a vivid [BACKDROP COLOR] seamless studio backdrop with a strong, intentional pose. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the body line that flatters the garment's silhouette, the light shaping (one sculpting key plus a hint of rim), and which side keeps clean negative space for type. THEN GENERATE: place the subject off-center with a striking [POSE/ATTITUDE] that shows the garment's cut and movement; light with a directional key that carves form, a gentle fill, and a subtle edge light separating subject from background; keep fabric drape, seams and color tack-sharp; shoot full or three-quarter length with a slightly wide-normal lens; grade for a bold, contemporary editorial palette. CONSTRAINTS: keep the garment's real shape, fabric and color believable; realistic anatomy and natural hands, no floating limbs, no melted textiles or warped seams, no added text or logos, one subject only, lighting that looks like real strobes (no flat AI glow). OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished studio editorial image only in 4:5, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the pose or the backdrop color.
h: Describe the [SUBJECT], name the [OUTFIT], pick a [BACKDROP COLOR], and optionally the [POSE/ATTITUDE].

p: ROLE: You are a street-style fashion photographer capturing off-duty looks outside the shows. TASK: Photograph [SUBJECT] wearing [OUTFIT] candidly mid-walk in [CITY/STREET SETTING] as if caught between appointments. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the walking direction and stride, the believable gesture (adjusting a sleeve, glancing aside), and the time of day that gives the most flattering directional light. THEN GENERATE: capture a full-length candid with natural motion in the step and clothing; place the subject sharp against a softly blurred [CITY/STREET SETTING] with real architectural and street detail; light with directional [TIME OF DAY] daylight and gentle shadows; shoot at a normal focal length with a moderately shallow depth so the background falls away; keep the outfit's fabrics, layers and footwear accurate and crisp; feel candid and unposed, not stiff. CONSTRAINTS: realistic anatomy, natural five-finger hands, believable five-toe feet if visible, accurate garment construction (no fused seams), legible-but-unbranded surroundings (no fake logos or text), one subject only, authentic daylight (no studio look). OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished street-style image only in 4:5, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the city setting or the time of day.
h: Describe the [SUBJECT], name the [OUTFIT], set the [CITY/STREET SETTING], and optionally the [TIME OF DAY].

p: ROLE: You are a fashion stylist and still-life photographer shooting an overhead outfit flat-lay for a lookbook. TASK: Arrange [GARMENTS] together with [ACCESSORIES] as one cohesive styled outfit, photographed straight from directly above. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide how the pieces lay as if a person were wearing them (top above bottoms, shoes below), the spacing, and how to balance color across the frame so it reads as an intentional look. THEN GENERATE: shoot perfectly top-down on a flat [SURFACE/TEXTURE] surface; lay the garments naturally with soft, believable fabric folds and the accessories tucked in at tidy angles; light evenly and softly so shadows are gentle and consistent; keep every piece crisp, color-accurate within the [PALETTE], and well-separated with balanced negative space; make it feel like a complete, wearable outfit, not random clutter. CONSTRAINTS: keep each garment's real shape, fabric weight and color believable; true overhead angle with no perspective tilt; soft even light with no harsh hotspots; no overlapping mess, no added text or logos, no fake brand labels. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished flat-lay image only in 4:5 (or 1:1 if it composes better), then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the surface texture or swap a garment.
h: List the [GARMENTS] in the outfit, name any [ACCESSORIES], and optionally set the [SURFACE/TEXTURE] and [PALETTE].

p: ROLE: You are a streetwear campaign photographer shooting a drop-day hero frame with attitude. TASK: Photograph [SUBJECT] in [STREETWEAR FIT] in [SETTING] with the raw, confident energy of a hype brand launch. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the stance that reads as effortless confidence, whether the fit is oversized or tailored, and the [LIGHT STYLE] (hard direct flash vs. moody ambient neon) that suits the mood. THEN GENERATE: place the subject with a grounded, low-key powerful pose that shows the silhouette and layering of the fit; set them against a textured urban [SETTING] (concrete, steel, neon, signage shapes) softly out of focus; light with the chosen [LIGHT STYLE] so fabric texture, drawstrings, prints and sneaker detail pop; shoot wide-normal with deliberate, slightly cinematic grading and rich contrast; keep the streetwear pieces sharp and accurate. CONSTRAINTS: keep garment construction, prints and sneaker shapes believable; realistic anatomy and natural hands; no fake brand logos or readable copyrighted marks, no added text; authentic light (flash or ambient, not flat AI glow); one subject only. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished streetwear campaign image only in 4:5, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the setting or switch the lighting style.
h: Describe the [SUBJECT], name the [STREETWEAR FIT], pick the [SETTING] (e.g. parking garage, neon alley), and optionally the [LIGHT STYLE].

p: ROLE: You are a beauty editorial photographer shooting a tight cosmetics close-up for a magazine page. TASK: Render an extreme beauty close-up of [SUBJECT] wearing [MAKEUP LOOK], framed to showcase the makeup artistry. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the crop (full face vs. eyes-and-lips), the eyeline, and the light that flatters skin while revealing the makeup's color and finish. THEN GENERATE: fill the frame with a sharp, luminous beauty close-up; render skin with a [SKIN FINISH] (dewy or matte) showing real pores, soft peach fuzz and natural catchlights; make the [MAKEUP LOOK] precise and editorial — clean liner edges, defined lashes, true lip color, blended shadow; light with a soft beauty dish and subtle reflectors against a [BACKDROP COLOR] backdrop; shoot at a macro-portrait focal length with crisp focus on the eyes and a gentle fall-off. CONSTRAINTS: photoreal skin texture (no plastic smoothing), symmetrical natural eyes with correct lashes, accurate teeth if lips part, no smeared or melted makeup edges, no added text or logos, one face only. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished beauty close-up image only in 4:5 (or 1:1), then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the makeup look or the skin finish.
h: Describe the [SUBJECT], name the [MAKEUP LOOK] (e.g. graphic liner, dewy nude, bold red lip), and optionally the [SKIN FINISH] and [BACKDROP COLOR].

p: ROLE: You are an accessory and jewelry photographer shooting an on-model hero frame for an editorial campaign. TASK: Render [ACCESSORY] worn on [SUBJECT/BODY PART] so the accessory is the unmistakable focal point with the model supporting it. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the crop that hero's the piece (close on the hand, ear, neckline or face), the angle that flatters its shape, and the light that makes metal, stones or lenses catch and sparkle. THEN GENERATE: compose tight around the [ACCESSORY] worn naturally on [SUBJECT/BODY PART] with an elegant, relaxed pose; render the piece tack-sharp with accurate materials — true metal tone, faceted gem highlights, lens reflections or leather grain — while skin and surroundings fall softly out of focus; light with controlled directional light plus a small specular highlight to make the accessory gleam; set a clean [BACKDROP] and carry the [MOOD] through grade and styling. CONSTRAINTS: keep the accessory's real shape, material and proportions accurate; realistic skin and natural five-finger hands if a hand appears, correct ear/face anatomy; no warped metal, no extra fingers, no added text or logos; the accessory stays the clear hero. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished on-model accessory image only in 4:5, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the crop or the backdrop.
h: Name the [ACCESSORY], describe the [SUBJECT/BODY PART] wearing it (e.g. hand, ear, face), and optionally the [MOOD] and [BACKDROP].

p: ROLE: You are a cinematic fashion photographer shooting an atmospheric narrative editorial. TASK: Photograph [SUBJECT] in [OUTFIT] within [SCENE/LOCATION] as a moody, film-still-like fashion frame. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the single emotion the frame should convey, where the subject sits in the environment, and how light and atmosphere (fog, haze, rim light, window glow) carry the [MOOD]. THEN GENERATE: place the subject within a rich, evocative [SCENE/LOCATION] with real environmental detail and depth; introduce believable atmosphere — soft haze, [TIME/LIGHT] streaming in, gentle shadows — that wraps the figure; shoot with a cinematic aspect and a moderate telephoto for compression; grade with a cohesive filmic palette and a hint of grain; keep the outfit readable and sharp while the world has texture and falloff; pose the subject naturally within the story, not posed to camera. CONSTRAINTS: keep garments accurate; realistic anatomy and natural hands; atmosphere that looks physically real (no flat fog stickers); no added text or logos, no fake brand marks; one subject only; cohesive, intentional color grade. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished cinematic editorial image only in 3:2 (or 16:9), then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 3:2 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the location or shift the mood.
h: Describe the [SUBJECT], name the [OUTFIT], set the [SCENE/LOCATION], and optionally the [MOOD] and [TIME/LIGHT].

p: ROLE: You are an e-commerce apparel photographer producing a clean ghost-mannequin product image. TASK: Render [GARMENT] as if worn by an invisible body, holding a natural three-dimensional shape with no visible model, on a [BACKGROUND]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the [VIEW] (front or back), how the garment should fill out at shoulders, chest and hem, and where the inner collar or lining shows. THEN GENERATE: shoot the garment straight-on with the ghost-mannequin effect so it looks worn and filled (rounded shoulders, natural sleeve drape, structured hem) but the body is invisible; keep the [FABRIC NOTES] true — weave, sheen, stitching and color accurate; show the inner back collar where appropriate for a finished catalog look; light evenly and softly on a clean [BACKGROUND] with a faint, realistic contact shadow; keep edges crisp and easy to cut out. CONSTRAINTS: garment shape, fabric and color must stay accurate; no visible mannequin, hands or body parts; even shadow-free-but-grounded lighting; no warped seams or melted fabric; no added text or logos beyond what is genuinely on the garment. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished ghost-mannequin image only in 4:5 (or 1:1), then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to switch to the back view or change the background.
h: Name the [GARMENT], set the [BACKGROUND] (e.g. pure white), and optionally the [VIEW] (front/back) and [FABRIC NOTES].

p: ROLE: You are a lookbook photographer shooting clean full-length on-model frames for a fashion catalog. TASK: Photograph [SUBJECT] head-to-toe in the complete [OUTFIT] against a neutral [BACKDROP TONE], styled to show exactly how the look fits and falls. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide a relaxed [POSE] that keeps the full silhouette visible, the framing that includes the shoes with breathing room top and bottom, and even light that shows true fabric color. THEN GENERATE: frame the subject full-length, centered with clean margin above the head and below the feet; pose them in a natural, easy stance that reveals the garment's cut, layering and drape; light with soft, even studio light and a gentle floor shadow for grounding; keep fabrics, footwear and color accurate and sharp from head to toe; set against a smooth [BACKDROP TONE] seamless. CONSTRAINTS: realistic anatomy, natural five-finger hands, correct footwear shape; accurate garment construction and color; no cropped feet, no warped proportions, no added text or logos; one subject only; clean catalog lighting (no heavy mood grade). OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished full-length lookbook image only in 4:5 (or 2:3), then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 2:3 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the pose or the backdrop tone.
h: Describe the [SUBJECT], name the full [OUTFIT], set the [BACKDROP TONE], and optionally the [POSE].

p: ROLE: You are an avant-garde fashion art director creating a conceptual editorial that treats clothing as sculpture. TASK: Render [SUBJECT] in [CONCEPT/OUTFIT] inside [SURREAL SETTING] as a bold, boundary-pushing art-fashion image. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the one striking idea the image is built around, how the silhouette and set reinforce it, and how the [COLOR STORY] ties the surreal elements together without becoming chaotic. THEN GENERATE: pose the subject in a sculptural, expressive shape that emphasizes the garment's form and the concept; build a surreal yet cohesive [SURREAL SETTING] with intentional props, scale play or unexpected materials; commit fully to the [COLOR STORY] across set, light and styling; light dramatically to model the silhouette and create depth; keep the image deliberate and gallery-worthy — strange but composed, never messy. CONSTRAINTS: even when surreal, keep human anatomy plausible and hands natural; garments should read as designed pieces, not noise; one cohesive concept (no random clutter); no added text or logos, no fake brand marks; intentional, balanced composition. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished conceptual editorial image only in 4:5 (or 3:2), then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw --stylize 250'), then one sentence I can paste back to push the concept further or change the color story.
h: Describe the [SUBJECT], name the [CONCEPT/OUTFIT], set the [SURREAL SETTING], and optionally the [COLOR STORY].

p: ROLE: You are a fashion photographer recreating the look of a specific film era for a retro editorial. TASK: Photograph [SUBJECT] in [OUTFIT] styled and graded to feel authentically of the [ERA], as if shot on period film. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): recall the visual signatures of the [ERA] — the color palette, the hair and styling, the typical lens and grain — and decide how to make the frame read unmistakably as that decade. THEN GENERATE: style hair, makeup and outfit true to the [ERA]; pose the subject in a manner typical of that period's editorials; light and grade to match the decade's signature palette and contrast; emulate the chosen [FILM STOCK/GRAIN] with realistic grain, slight halation and period color shift; choose a focal length and depth consistent with the era's cameras; keep the outfit accurate while the whole frame feels like a found vintage scan. CONSTRAINTS: keep period styling believable and not a cartoon of the decade; realistic anatomy and natural hands; authentic film texture (grain and color, not a heavy filter overlay); no modern logos or anachronistic items; no added text; one subject only. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished vintage editorial image only in 4:5 (or 3:2), then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 3:2 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the era or the film stock.
h: Describe the [SUBJECT], name the [ERA] (e.g. 1970s, 1990s), name the [OUTFIT], and optionally the [FILM STOCK/GRAIN].

p: ROLE: You are a luxury still-life photographer shooting an overhead accessory flat-lay for an editorial detail page. TASK: Arrange [ACCESSORIES] in a refined top-down composition that makes each piece feel premium and collectible. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide which piece anchors the frame, how to group the rest by size and material, and the spacing that feels editorial rather than crowded. THEN GENERATE: shoot perfectly top-down on a tactile [SURFACE/TEXTURE]; arrange the accessories with deliberate, breathing-room spacing and a clear hero; render materials accurately — gleaming metal, true glass reflections, fine leather grain, fragrance-bottle clarity; light with soft raking light that carves out texture and adds small specular highlights to metal and glass; keep the [PALETTE] cohesive and the [LIGHT MOOD] consistent; balance negative space so the eye moves through the pieces. CONSTRAINTS: keep each item's real shape, material and proportions accurate; true overhead angle with no perspective tilt; believable reflections and shadows (no fake glow); no overlapping clutter, no added text or logos, no invented brand marks. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished accessory flat-lay image only in 4:5 (or 1:1), then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 1:1 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the surface texture or swap an accessory.
h: List the [ACCESSORIES], set the [SURFACE/TEXTURE], and optionally the [PALETTE] and [LIGHT MOOD].

p: ROLE: You are a commercial product photographer who writes natural-language prompts for FLUX.1 (which reads flowing sentences, not Midjourney comma-keyword stacks, and renders quoted text reliably). GOAL: Turn my brief into ONE photoreal, listing-ready e-commerce hero image. INPUTS — Product: [PRODUCT]. Exact label text to render: [BRAND/LABEL TEXT]. Aspect ratio: [ASPECT RATIO]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not show this): identify the product's real material and finish, decide where the key light must sit to reveal that texture, and how to keep the label front-facing and unwarped. THEN write ONE flowing descriptive paragraph that names: the product with its exact material and finish; a clean seamless studio sweep with a soft gradient; a three-point lighting setup (large softbox key at 45 degrees, subtle rim/separation light, gentle fill) with one controlled specular highlight; a shallow-depth-of-field 100mm macro look; true-to-life color, accurate reflections and realistic micro-texture; and the label reading the exact words "[BRAND/LABEL TEXT]" in a clean sans-serif, sharp and correctly spelled. CONSTRAINTS: photoreal only; no extra props; no warped, doubled, or gibberish text; physically plausible reflections. Express the aspect ratio in plain words inside the paragraph (e.g. "square 1:1 composition", "vertical 4:5 frame") — FLUX.1 does not use --ar flags. OUTPUT FORMAT (exactly, nothing else): 1) PROMPT: <one copy-paste paragraph, ending with the stated aspect ratio in words> 2) TEXT TIP: <one line on keeping "[BRAND/LABEL TEXT]" legible in FLUX, e.g. shorten or quote it>
h: Name the [PRODUCT] and its material; set the exact [BRAND/LABEL TEXT] and an [ASPECT RATIO].

p: ROLE: You are an architectural and signage photographer writing for FLUX.1, which renders legible text best when you quote it and keep it short. THINK FIRST (silently, do not show): pick the time of day and weather that flatter this shopfront, and place the sign so every word stays readable head-on. THEN write ONE descriptive paragraph naming: the shopfront and its real materials (brick, glass, painted wood, metal trim); a fascia or hanging sign reading exactly "[SIGN TEXT]" in a fitting typeface at high contrast; the lighting (golden-hour side light or warm evening window glow); realistic reflections in the glass; a wide 24mm street perspective; a clean, people-free foreground; fine surface texture and natural weathering; shot on a full-frame camera at f/4. CONSTRAINTS: spelling exact; no duplicated, floating, or melted letters; no cartoon look; no crowds. State the aspect ratio in words in the sentence — FLUX.1 uses no --ar flag. OUTPUT FORMAT (exactly, nothing else): 1) PROMPT: <one copy-paste paragraph, ending "in a wide 3:2 landscape frame"> 2) SIGN FIX: <one line on what to do if a letter renders wrong> 3) NIGHT VARIANT: <one sentence swapping the scene to neon-lit dusk>
h: Give the [BUSINESS TYPE], the exact [SIGN TEXT], and an optional [CITY/STREET VIBE].

p: ROLE: You are a fashion and editorial photographer writing a single natural-language FLUX.1 prompt (flowing sentences, no keyword spam). GOAL: One photoreal, magazine-cover-grade portrait. INPUTS — Subject: [SUBJECT]. Mood / story: [MOOD/STORY]. Wardrobe and color: [WARDROBE/COLOR]. THEN write ONE rich descriptive paragraph naming: the subject and exact expression; a clear setting or seamless backdrop; an explicit lighting plan (e.g. soft beauty-dish key, subtle hair light, deep shadow falloff for drama); realistic skin texture with visible pores and natural imperfections (no plastic over-smoothing); the stated wardrobe and color palette; a named lens and aperture (85mm at f/1.4 for compression and creamy bokeh); eye-level framing; a neutral-to-cinematic color grade; shot on a full-frame mirrorless body. CONSTRAINTS: photoreal; true human anatomy; correct hands, eyes and teeth; no AI gloss; no text anywhere. State the aspect ratio in words — FLUX.1 has no --ar flag. OUTPUT FORMAT (exactly, nothing else): 1) PROMPT: <one copy-paste paragraph, ending "as a vertical 4:5 editorial frame"> 2) RIM-LIGHT SWAP: <one line for a moodier low-key version> 3) LENS NOTE: <one line on what changes if you switch to 35mm>
h: Describe the [SUBJECT], the [MOOD/STORY], and a [WARDROBE/COLOR] direction.

p: ROLE: You are a packaging designer and product-shot retoucher writing for FLUX.1, which renders short quoted text cleanly. GOAL: One photoreal packaging mockup with a readable text hierarchy. THEN write ONE descriptive paragraph naming: the package format and material with realistic finish and subtle edge highlights; the front panel showing "[BRAND NAME]" as the dominant logotype, "[PRODUCT NAME]" beneath it, and "[TAGLINE]" in smaller supporting type; a clean studio surface with a soft reflection; a softbox key plus gentle gradient background; a 50mm product-photography perspective at f/8 for edge-to-edge panel sharpness; accurate color and micro-texture. CONSTRAINTS: all three text lines spelled exactly with clear size hierarchy; no gibberish small print; no second package; no warped panel. State the aspect ratio in words — FLUX.1 takes no --ar flag. OUTPUT FORMAT (exactly, nothing else): 1) PROMPT: <one copy-paste paragraph, ending "in a square 1:1 frame"> 2) HIERARCHY TIP: <one line on type sizing for legibility> 3) PALETTE: <one line suggesting a 2-3 color scheme that fits the category>
h: Name the [PACKAGE FORMAT], the [BRAND NAME], the [PRODUCT NAME] and a [TAGLINE].

p: ROLE: You are a prop stylist and flat-lay photographer writing one natural-language FLUX.1 prompt. GOAL: One photoreal, perfectly top-down lifestyle flat lay. INPUTS — Hero item: [HERO ITEM]. Theme and palette: [THEME/PALETTE]. Small card text to render exactly: [CARD TEXT]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not show): choose the 4-6 props that support the hero without clutter, and decide where the breathing room (negative space) sits. THEN write ONE descriptive paragraph naming: a true 90-degree overhead composition on a textured surface fitting the theme; the hero item placed on a rule-of-thirds anchor; 4-6 complementary props arranged with deliberate negative space; a small card or tag reading exactly "[CARD TEXT]" in tidy handwriting or clean type; soft, even diffused daylight from one side casting gentle shadows; realistic material textures (fabric weave, paper grain, ceramic glaze); shot on a 35mm lens at f/5.6; true color in the stated palette. CONSTRAINTS: balanced layout; exact card spelling; nothing floating or clipping; photoreal. State the aspect ratio in words — FLUX.1 has no --ar flag. h: Set the [HERO ITEM], the [THEME/PALETTE], and the short [CARD TEXT] to render.

p: ROLE: You are a cinematographer writing one natural-language FLUX.1 prompt for a neo-noir car portrait. GOAL: One photoreal frame that looks like a still from a modern noir film. INPUTS — Subject: [SUBJECT IN THE CAR]. City and neon colors: [CITY/NEON COLORS]. Time and weather: [TIME/WEATHER]. THEN write ONE moody descriptive paragraph naming: the subject seen through a rain-streaked side window of a parked car; neon signage reflected and refracted across the wet glass; dramatic low-key lighting with deep shadows and a colored rim from the neon; a shallow 50mm f/1.8 look with the face in sharp focus and bokeh city lights behind; a cinematic teal-and-magenta grade; fine film grain; realistic skin and water-droplet detail. CONSTRAINTS: photoreal; correct anatomy and hands; no text; no over-saturation that crushes shadow detail. State the aspect ratio in words — FLUX.1 has no --ar flag. OUTPUT FORMAT (exactly, nothing else): 1) PROMPT: <one copy-paste paragraph, ending "as a vertical 4:5 cinematic frame"> 2) MOOD DIAL: <one line to push it warmer or colder> 3) REFLECTION TIP: <one line on selling the wet-glass realism>
h: Describe the [SUBJECT IN THE CAR], the [CITY/NEON COLORS] and an optional [TIME/WEATHER].

p: ROLE: You are a poster and graphic designer writing one FLUX.1 prompt where text accuracy is the top priority (FLUX renders short quoted phrases best). CONSTRAINTS: spell all text exactly; no extra random words; no clashing fonts; print-ready look. State the aspect ratio in words — FLUX.1 has no --ar flag. OUTPUT FORMAT (exactly, nothing else): 1) PROMPT: <one copy-paste paragraph, ending "as a vertical 2:3 poster"> 2) FALLBACK: <one line on simplifying if the long text garbles> 3) VARIANT: <one sentence for a minimalist version of the same poster>
h: Give the [EVENT/HEADLINE], the [DATE + VENUE], a [STYLE/ERA], and a [2-COLOR PALETTE].

p: ROLE: You are a food photographer and stylist writing one natural-language FLUX.1 prompt. GOAL: One photoreal, appetite-triggering hero plate. INPUTS — Dish: [DISH]. Cuisine and mood: [CUISINE/MOOD]. Menu card text to render exactly: [MENU CARD TEXT]. CONSTRAINTS: photoreal and edible-looking; exact card spelling; no plastic sheen; no extra plates. State the aspect ratio in words — FLUX.1 has no --ar flag. p: ROLE: You are an interiors and architectural photographer writing one FLUX.1 prompt. GOAL: One photoreal, MLS-grade interior listing shot. INPUTS — Room and style: [ROOM/STYLE]. Time of day: [TIME OF DAY]. Framed wall-art text to render exactly: [FRAMED WALL TEXT]. CONSTRAINTS: no warped or bowed walls; exact framed-text spelling; no fisheye distortion; no clutter. State the aspect ratio in words — FLUX.1 has no --ar flag. OUTPUT FORMAT (exactly, nothing else): 1) PROMPT: <one copy-paste paragraph, ending "as a wide 3:2 landscape frame"> 2) LIGHT TIP: <one line on avoiding blown-out windows> 3) MOOD SWAP: <one sentence for a cozy evening-lit version>

p: ROLE: You are a toy-product photographer writing one natural-language FLUX.1 prompt that preserves the subject's identity. Name on the box: [YOUR NAME]. Tagline: [TAGLINE]. Accessories: [3 ACCESSORIES]. p: ROLE: You are a film-photography colorist writing one natural-language FLUX.1 prompt that fights the over-clean AI look. GOAL: One authentically imperfect 2000s disposable-camera photo from the uploaded selfie. INPUTS — Restyle the uploaded selfie as a 2000s disposable-camera snapshot while keeping the same person, pose and expression recognizable. Scene / vibe: [SCENE/VIBE]. Date stamp to render exactly: [DATE STAMP]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not show): choose the film flaws that sell it as a real memory without hiding the face. THEN write ONE descriptive paragraph naming: a point-and-shoot disposable-camera look with harsh direct on-camera flash; visible film grain and slight motion softness; warm faded color with a mild light leak in one corner; the stated scene and vibe around the subject; a white Polaroid-style border; a small orange-yellow date stamp in the lower-right reading exactly "[DATE STAMP]"; photoreal skin with natural imperfections (no smoothing); as if shot at night on cheap 400-speed film.
