OUTPUT FORMAT (exactly, nothing else): 1) PROMPT: <one copy-paste paragraph, ending "as a vertical 4:5 frame"> 2) GRAIN DIAL: <one line to push it grittier or cleaner> 3) DECADE SWAP: <one sentence to retime it to the 1980s or Y2K>
h: Upload the selfie; set the [SCENE/VIBE] and the exact [DATE STAMP] to print.

p: ROLE: You are an out-of-home advertising art director writing one FLUX.1 prompt. GOAL: One photoreal out-of-home ad shown believably in context. INPUTS — Brand: [BRAND]. Ad headline to render exactly: [AD HEADLINE]. Placement: [PLACEMENT/LOCATION] (highway billboard, subway platform, bus shelter, building wall, etc.). Palette: [PALETTE]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not show): pick the camera angle that keeps the ad readable in context, and the lighting that matches the location and time of day. THEN write ONE descriptive paragraph naming: the chosen placement shown in a believable real-world environment with correct perspective and scale; the ad panel displaying "[AD HEADLINE]" as the dominant headline plus the "[BRAND]" logo; the stated palette driving strong contrast and brand feel; realistic environmental lighting and shadows matching the time of day; fine texture on surfaces (concrete, vinyl, glass); a wide 24mm street perspective; photoreal materials and reflections; shot on a full-frame camera at f/5.6. CONSTRAINTS: spell the headline and brand exactly; keep the ad panel flat and legible; no warped letters; no crowds blocking the panel. State the aspect ratio in words — FLUX.1 has no --ar flag. OUTPUT FORMAT (exactly, nothing else): 1) PROMPT: <one copy-paste paragraph, ending "as a wide 16:9 frame"> 2) READABILITY TIP: <one line on keeping the headline crisp at this scale> 3) PLACEMENT SWAP: <one sentence moving the ad to a different format>
h: Give the [BRAND], the exact [AD HEADLINE], the [PLACEMENT/LOCATION], and an optional [PALETTE].

p: ROLE: You are an infographic designer writing one FLUX.1 prompt where every label must render correctly (FLUX handles short quoted labels best). GOAL: One clean, social-shareable how-to infographic card. INPUTS — Topic: [TOPIC/RECIPE]. Heading to render exactly: [HEADING]. Step labels to render in order: [3-5 STEP LABELS]. Brand palette: [BRAND PALETTE]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not show): decide how to space the steps so text stays sharp, and which simple icons reinforce each step. THEN write ONE descriptive paragraph naming: a clean modern infographic card with the heading "[HEADING]" set boldly at the top; a vertical or grid layout of numbered steps, each showing one of "[3-5 STEP LABELS]" beside a simple matching icon or illustration; the brand palette applied consistently with strong contrast for legibility; generous white space and tidy alignment; a flat vector look with subtle depth; crisp, readable typography throughout. CONSTRAINTS: spell the heading and every step label exactly; keep numbers in correct order; no random extra text; no clashing colors; print-clean finish. State the aspect ratio in words — FLUX.1 has no --ar flag. OUTPUT FORMAT (exactly, nothing else): 1) PROMPT: <one copy-paste paragraph, ending "as a vertical 4:5 card"> 2) LEGIBILITY TIP: <one line on keeping small step text sharp in FLUX> 3) STYLE SWAP: <one sentence for a dark-mode version of the same card>
h: Set the [TOPIC/RECIPE], the exact [HEADING], the [3-5 STEP LABELS], and a [BRAND PALETTE].

p: ROLE: You are a professional food photographer shooting a hero plate for a restaurant's flagship menu and ad campaign. TASK: Render the [DISH] as a single mouth-watering hero shot, freshly plated and at its most appetising moment. THINK FIRST (do this silently, do not print it): decide the camera angle that flatters this dish's height and shape (45-degree three-quarter for layered plates, top-down for flat compositions), where the freshest glistening highlight should fall, and which one ingredient should sit in razor-sharp focus. THEN GENERATE: plate the dish on a [SURFACE] with intentional negative space on one side for a headline; light it with one large soft key from the back-left for appetising rim highlights and gentle directional shadow, plus a subtle fill to keep textures readable; show the food at peak freshness — glossy sauces, crisp edges, a faint wisp of natural steam if hot, condensation if cold — with a shallow depth of field that holds the hero element tack-sharp and lets the background fall into a creamy blur; keep colours vivid but true to real food. CONSTRAINTS: keep ingredients realistic and edible-looking — no plastic sheen, no melted or warped shapes, no impossible garnishes; one plate as the clear hero, props minimal and supporting only; no added text or logos; natural food colours (no neon AI saturation); steam and droplets physically believable. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished hero food image only in [ASPECT RATIO], then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw --v 6'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the angle, surface or garnish.
h: Name the [DISH] (and a key ingredient or two), optionally set a [SURFACE] (e.g. dark slate, rustic oak) and the platform [ASPECT RATIO].

p: ROLE: You are a beverage photographer shooting an irresistibly refreshing drink shot. TASK: Capture the [DRINK] in its glass at the exact moment it looks most thirst-quenching. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide whether cold-and-sweating or warm-and-glowing suits this drink, the light direction that makes the liquid translucent, and where bubbles, ice or garnish should catch a highlight. THEN GENERATE: place the glass slightly off-centre against a clean [BACKDROP TONE] with a soft gradient; backlight or side-light the liquid so it glows translucently and the [GARNISH] reads crisply; for cold drinks add fresh beaded condensation and a slow drip down the glass, clear ice with internal sparkle, and rising bubbles caught mid-stream; for warm drinks add a faint wisp of steam and a cozy glow; keep the rim, surface and any foam or crema photoreal and tack-sharp with a shallow depth of field and gentle bokeh behind. CONSTRAINTS: keep the liquid colour and clarity true to the real drink; physically believable condensation, bubbles and ice (no floating impossible droplets, no smeared shapes); clean glass with realistic refraction; no added text or logos; one drink as the hero. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished drink image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the garnish, backdrop or temperature mood.
h: Name the [DRINK] and its glass, list any [GARNISH] (e.g. lime wheel, mint, salt rim), and optionally a [BACKDROP TONE].

p: ROLE: You are a food stylist shooting a generous overhead table spread for social media. TASK: Arrange a [CUISINE OR MEAL] spread photographed straight from above, centred on the [MAIN DISH]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the hero bowl or plate, how to balance colour and spacing so the eye lands on it first, and where to leave a little breathing room so the frame doesn't feel cramped. THEN GENERATE: shoot perfectly top-down with the [MAIN DISH] as the clear centrepiece, surrounded by the [SIDES & PROPS] — small bowls, sauces, fresh herbs, cutlery, napkins and a few scattered raw ingredients — arranged with deliberate, slightly organic spacing on a [SURFACE]; light it softly and evenly so shadows are gentle and consistent; keep every dish crisp, well-separated and colour-accurate, with an abundant, inviting rhythm and one or two hands reaching in if it adds life. CONSTRAINTS: keep each dish realistic and appetising; true overhead angle with no perspective tilt; if a hand appears, show natural five-finger anatomy; even soft lighting (no harsh hotspots); no added text or logos; props support the food, never bury it. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished flat-lay image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 1:1 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the surface, swap a side, or add/remove the reaching hand.
h: Name the [CUISINE OR MEAL] and its [MAIN DISH], list the [SIDES & PROPS] to scatter around it, and optionally a [SURFACE].

p: ROLE: You are a macro food photographer who sells flavour through extreme detail. TASK: Shoot an ultra-close macro frame of the [FOOD] that heroes its [DETAIL]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the exact spot that proves freshness or craft, the angle that reveals texture, and the light that rakes across the surface to carve out depth. THEN GENERATE: fill the frame with a tight macro view of the chosen [DETAIL] — show fine surface texture, glistening moisture, crumb, grain, char or juice in razor-sharp focus, with a beautifully soft fall-off blur just behind the focal plane; light it with [LIGHT MOOD] raking from the side so highlights and micro-shadows pop and the food looks tactile and alive; keep colours true and the surface appetising enough to almost taste. CONSTRAINTS: keep the texture and colour authentic to the real food — photoreal micro-detail, no plastic-smooth AI sheen, no over-saturation; believable raking light; one focal detail only; no added text or logos. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished macro image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to move the focus to a different detail or change the light mood.
h: Name the [FOOD], pick the [DETAIL] to feature (e.g. melting cheese pull, flaky pastry layers, glistening caviar), and optionally the [LIGHT MOOD].

p: ROLE: You are a high-speed food and beverage photographer specialising in frozen-motion shots. TASK: Capture the [SUBJECT] in a dramatic mid-action moment, with the [ACTION] frozen as if caught by a flash. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the direction of the pour or splash, which droplets and pieces orbit the subject, and the light that makes liquid and surfaces sparkle. THEN GENERATE: place the [SUBJECT] as the sharp hero, surrounded by a crisp frozen splash, flying droplets and any suspended ingredients caught in crystal-clear high-speed detail; set it against a clean [BACKDROP COLOR] with a subtle gradient; light it with hard directional flash plus rim light so every droplet and edge sparkles; keep a strong sense of energy and motion while the food or drink stays perfectly readable and appetising. CONSTRAINTS: keep colours and shapes true to the real food; physically believable splash, pour and droplet behaviour (no smeared, melted or floating-wrong shapes); photoreal liquid surfaces; no added text or logos; one clear hero subject. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished frozen-motion image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the action, flying elements or backdrop colour.
h: Name the [SUBJECT] and the [ACTION] (e.g. pouring milk, dropping berries, splashing juice), and optionally a [BACKDROP COLOR].

p: ROLE: You are a fine-art food photographer shooting a moody, painterly scene for a premium cookbook. TASK: Stage the [DISH] in a rustic dark-and-dramatic composition with chiaroscuro lighting. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide where the single soft window beam falls, which textures it should reveal, and how much of the frame should drop into rich shadow to feel artisanal, not murky. THEN GENERATE: place the [DISH] in soft directional light as if from a single window, against a dark moody background of weathered wood, dark stone or deep linen; add a few tasteful [PROPS] — rough fabric, cast iron, scattered herbs, crumbs — and a faint wisp of steam carrying the [SEASON/MOOD]; let highlights glisten on the food while the surroundings fall into deep, controlled shadow with warm low-key tones; keep the hero food sharp, richly textured and appetising, with intentional negative space and a painterly, Old-Master feel. CONSTRAINTS: keep food realistic and edible-looking; rich blacks but the dish never lost to darkness; believable single-source lighting (no flat AI glow); natural colours warmed by the light, not artificially saturated; no added text or logos; tasteful, uncluttered styling. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished moody food image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to lift the shadows, change the props or shift the season.
h: Name the [DISH], optionally set the [PROPS] (e.g. linen, cast iron, fresh herbs) and a [SEASON/MOOD].

p: ROLE: You are a lifestyle food photographer shooting a bright, fresh, health-forward image. TASK: Render the [DISH] in a clean, airy, sunlit scene that feels wholesome and energising. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the time of day for the cleanest daylight, which fresh ingredients add a pop of [ACCENT COLOR], and how to keep the frame light and uncluttered. THEN GENERATE: place the [DISH] on a light surface within [SETTING], bathed in soft, bright natural daylight with gentle shadows and an airy high-key feel; scatter a few fresh whole ingredients — leaves, fruit, seeds — for colour and authenticity; keep whites clean, greens vivid and the food crisp, dewy and just-prepared; use a shallow depth of field so the dish stays sharp while the bright background softens, leaving calm negative space. CONSTRAINTS: keep food fresh, realistic and appetising — no wilting, no plastic gloss; clean true-to-life colours (bright but not blown out or neon); believable soft daylight; no added text or logos; uncluttered, breezy styling. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished fresh lifestyle image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the setting, lighten the mood further, or swap an ingredient.
h: Name the [DISH], optionally set the [SETTING] (e.g. sunlit kitchen, bright cafe table) and an [ACCENT COLOR] from the ingredients.

p: ROLE: You are an editorial food photographer capturing the human craft behind a dish. TASK: Show a pair of hands performing the [ACTION] with the [DISH OR FOOD], caught mid-moment as if for a story spread. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the one gesture that best shows care and craft, the angle that keeps both the hands and the food readable, and the light that makes the food the focal point. THEN GENERATE: frame the [DISH OR FOOD] as the clear hero with realistic hands mid-[ACTION] — drizzling, sprinkling, garnishing, kneading or pouring — within a softly blurred [SETTING] of a real kitchen or counter; light it with warm directional window light, gentle shadows and a candid, unstaged feel; keep the food and the active gesture sharp and appetising while the background falls into a creamy shallow-depth blur; capture a falling sprinkle, drip or wisp of steam to add life and motion. CONSTRAINTS: show natural five-finger hands with believable skin and no extra fingers; keep food realistic and appetising; authentic candid lighting (no stiff stock-photo posing); no added text or logos; one clear action. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished hands-in-action image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the action, setting or time of day.
h: Name the [DISH OR FOOD], describe the [ACTION] the hands are doing, and optionally the [SETTING].

p: ROLE: You are a commercial packaging photographer shooting a premium food product for retail and ads. TASK: Render the [PACKAGED PRODUCT] as a hero packshot styled on a believable set with its real ingredients. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the angle that flatters the package shape and shows the front label clearly, where to place the supporting ingredients, and how to keep the package the obvious hero. THEN GENERATE: place the [PACKAGED PRODUCT] front-and-centre with the label crisp and legible, styled on a [SURFACE/PALETTE] with the [INGREDIENTS] artfully arranged nearby — beans, fruit, grain, spilled product — to signal flavour and quality; light it with a soft key plus a clean edge highlight to separate it from the background, add a natural contact shadow and a subtle surface reflection; keep the packaging materials photoreal (matte, gloss, foil, glass, kraft paper) and tack-sharp with a shallow depth of field, leaving calm negative space for a headline. CONSTRAINTS: keep the package's real shape, colours and any visible branding accurate — do not invent a different product; keep ingredients realistic; no warped or melted labels; no added text or logos beyond what's on the package; one product as the hero; believable studio lighting. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished packaging image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the surface, swap an ingredient or adjust the angle.
h: Name the [PACKAGED PRODUCT] (e.g. coffee bag, jam jar, chocolate bar), list the [INGREDIENTS] to style around it, and optionally a [SURFACE/PALETTE].

p: ROLE: You are a food photographer producing a clean, consistent step-by-step recipe sequence. TASK: Create one image laid out as a tidy grid of matched panels showing the [KEY STEPS] of the [RECIPE] in order. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide how many panels the steps need, keep camera angle, distance, surface and lighting identical across every panel so the sequence reads as one set, and decide which gesture best shows each step. THEN GENERATE: render the steps as equal-sized panels in reading order, each shot from the same overhead or three-quarter angle on the same [SURFACE] with identical soft, even lighting; show the food progressing realistically from raw to finished — mixing, chopping, layering, cooking, plating — with hands acting where it helps; keep each panel crisp, appetising and colour-consistent, separated by thin clean dividers, with small legible step numbers in a clean sans-serif corner badge. CONSTRAINTS: keep food and progression realistic and continuous (the dish must look like the same recipe throughout); identical framing and light across panels; correctly rendered numbers only, no other text; natural five-finger hands if shown; no logos. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished recipe-grid image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 1:1 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the number of steps, the surface or the camera angle.
h: Name the [RECIPE], list the [KEY STEPS] in order (3-6 of them), and optionally a [SURFACE].

p: ROLE: You are a food commercial cinematographer framing a single still from a premium food film. TASK: Render one cinematic frame of the [DISH] caught at its [HERO MOMENT]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the moment of peak appetite appeal, the lens feel (shallow depth, gentle compression) and the colour grade that gives it a polished commercial mood. THEN GENERATE: frame the [DISH] with a shallow cinematic depth of field and soft, buttery key light, capturing the [HERO MOMENT] mid-action — a slow cheese pull, a glossy drizzle in motion, rising steam or a pour caught mid-stream; render warm bokeh in the background, gentle film grain and a [COLOR GRADE] that feels rich and intentional; keep the food tack-sharp at the focal plane, appetising and glistening, with a widescreen, story-driven composition. CONSTRAINTS: keep food realistic and appetising; physically believable motion (pulls, drips and steam that obey gravity); cinematic but natural colour (no over-graded neon); no added text or logos; one clear hero moment. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished cinematic food frame only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 16:9 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the hero moment or the colour grade.
h: Name the [DISH], describe the [HERO MOMENT] (e.g. cheese pull, sauce drizzle, steam rising), and optionally a [COLOR GRADE].

p: ROLE: You are a pastry and dessert photographer shooting an indulgent beauty shot for a bakery. TASK: Render the [DESSERT] as a decadent hero close-up that maximises temptation. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the angle that shows layers, height and gloss, where the [INDULGENT DETAIL] should catch the light, and how to make it look fresh and just-finished. THEN GENERATE: frame the [DESSERT] close and slightly above the surface against a soft [BACKDROP], showing the [INDULGENT DETAIL] — a slow molten drip, glossy glaze, a falling dust of powdered sugar or cocoa, a spoonful breaking into a soft centre — caught at its most tempting; light it with a soft directional key for glossy highlights and gentle shadow, plus a touch of backlight to make sugar dust and glaze sparkle; keep textures rich and tactile (crumb, cream, ganache, flake) and tack-sharp with a luscious shallow depth of field. CONSTRAINTS: keep the dessert realistic and edible-looking — believable drips, dust and glaze that obey gravity, no plastic gloss; natural rich colours (not artificially saturated); no added text or logos; one indulgent hero. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished dessert image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the indulgent detail, backdrop or angle.
h: Name the [DESSERT], describe the [INDULGENT DETAIL] (e.g. chocolate drip, powdered-sugar dust, fruit coulis), and optionally a [BACKDROP].

p: ROLE: You are an art director designing a ready-to-run static food ad creative. TASK: Build one balanced ad layout that pairs an appetising shot of the [DISH OR PRODUCT] with clean space reserved for a headline and an offer badge. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide which side holds the food and which holds the copy, how the [BRAND COLOR] should anchor the design, and where an [OFFER] badge would sit without crowding the food. THEN GENERATE: place a mouth-watering, well-lit shot of the [DISH OR PRODUCT] on one half of the frame against a clean background tinted toward [BRAND COLOR]; reserve a calm, uncluttered zone on the other half for a headline, plus a clear circular or pill-shaped area where an offer badge will go; light the food with a soft key and appetising highlights, add a natural contact shadow, and keep the overall composition balanced, on-brand and campaign-ready with deliberate negative space. CONSTRAINTS: keep the food realistic and appetising; leave the headline and badge zones genuinely empty (no placeholder lorem text, no invented logos or fake percentages); brand-colour background must not bleed onto the food; one hero dish; believable lighting. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished ad-layout image only in [ASPECT RATIO], then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 1:1 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to flip the layout, change the brand colour or resize the offer-badge zone.
h: Name the [DISH OR PRODUCT], set a [BRAND COLOR], optionally note the [OFFER] the badge should leave room for, and the [ASPECT RATIO].

p: ROLE: You are a senior magazine art director compositing a newsstand cover. Using the attached photo, place this exact person as the cover star of a glossy premium magazine in a tall portrait (3:4) layout. THINK FIRST (silently): decide masthead placement, where the subject's head overlaps the title, and which cover lines sit left vs right so nothing crowds the face. Render at the top a large bold masthead reading exactly "[MAGAZINE NAME]". Add a striking main cover headline reading exactly "[COVER HEADLINE]" and a smaller supporting line reading exactly "[SECONDARY LINE]", plus 2-3 short realistic teaser lines, an issue date, a price and a small barcode in the bottom corner. Light the subject like a studio cover shoot: soft beauty key light, gentle rim light, clean catchlights, flattering but real. CRITICAL: keep the same face, identity, skin tone, age and natural features unmistakably recognizable; do not slim or beautify into a different person. CONSTRAINTS: photoreal skin with texture (no plastic airbrush), all cover text crisp, correctly spelled and legibly kerned (no gibberish letters), masthead partially behind the head for depth, balanced editorial layout, one person only. OUTPUT: the finished cover image only.
h: Upload a clear photo, then set the [MAGAZINE NAME], a bold [COVER HEADLINE] and an optional [SECONDARY LINE].

p: ROLE: You are a product photographer shooting a commercial collectible figure. Using the attached photo, transform this person into a commercialized 1/7 scale PVC figurine displayed on a round transparent acrylic base. Keep the same face, hairstyle, outfit colors and proportions so the figurine is clearly recognizable as them, optionally restyled to this theme: [POSE OR THEME]. THINK FIRST (silently): position the figure, the monitor and the box so all three read clearly without clutter. Place the figurine on a real desk. Behind it, show a computer monitor displaying the 3D modeling viewport (wireframe/sculpt view) of this exact figurine; beside it place a premium Japanese figure-brand product box with the heading "[BRAND NAME]" printed cleanly on the front and a window showing the figure. Style: realistic indoor product photography, soft diffused desk light, shallow depth of field, glossy painted PVC surface. CONSTRAINTS: photoreal plastic, accurate five-finger hands, legible box text (no gibberish), the figure on screen and on the base must match, no warped faces, one figure only. OUTPUT: the edited image only.
h: Upload a full or half-body photo, then optionally set a [POSE OR THEME] and a [BRAND NAME] for the box.

p: ROLE: You are a vintage portrait retoucher emulating 1970s Indian film photography. Using the attached photo, restyle this person into an elegant vintage Indian studio portrait in the style of a 1970s Bollywood film poster. Dress them in [ATTIRE] with tasteful traditional jewelry that suits the outfit. Place them against [BACKDROP] with soft painterly studio lighting, a warm golden rim light and gentle film grain. CRITICAL: keep the exact same face, facial features, skin tone, age and expression so they remain unmistakably recognizable; do not slim, lighten or alter the face. Only change attire, hair styling, lighting and background. Add the subtle color cast and soft focus of vintage film. CONSTRAINTS: photoreal, no plastic skin, natural-looking jewelry and fabric folds, accurate hands, no text on the image, preserve the original head pose and gaze, one person only. OUTPUT: the edited image only.
h: Upload a clear face photo, then set the [ATTIRE] (e.g. royal blue silk saree) and a [BACKDROP] (e.g. palace courtyard).

p: ROLE: You are a professional corporate portrait photographer. Using the attached photo, turn this casual selfie into a polished LinkedIn headshot. Keep the exact same face, identity, skin tone, age and natural features fully recognizable. Dress the person in [ATTIRE], tidy the hair neatly and place them against [BACKDROP]. Light it like a real headshot session: soft key light from front-left, gentle fill, subtle catchlights in the eyes, clean separation from the background, shallow depth of field. Make the expression confident and approachable with a slight natural smile if the original allows. CONSTRAINTS: photoreal skin with visible pores (no airbrushed plastic look), do not change face shape or make them look like a different person, keep blemishes minimal but natural, no over-smoothing, no text or logos, head-and-shoulders crop. OUTPUT: the edited image only.
h: Upload a clear selfie, then set the [ATTIRE] (e.g. navy blazer) and a [BACKDROP] (e.g. soft grey studio).

p: ROLE: You are a corporate portrait photographer shooting an executive headshot — of an animal. Using the attached photo, create a professional corporate headshot of this pet as if it were a company executive. Keep the same animal, breed, fur color, markings and facial features clearly recognizable. Dress it in [OUTFIT] and place it against a softly blurred modern office or grey studio backdrop. Light it like a real LinkedIn headshot: soft front key light, gentle fill, catchlights in the eyes, shallow depth of field, dignified and slightly serious expression. Optionally add a small desk nameplate reading exactly "[JOB TITLE]" with crisp legible text. CONSTRAINTS: photoreal fur and fabric, the clothing must sit naturally on the animal's body, no extra limbs, no human face, keep it charming and believable rather than cartoonish, one animal only. OUTPUT: the edited image only.
h: Upload a clear photo of your pet, then set the [OUTFIT] (e.g. tiny grey suit and tie) and a [JOB TITLE] for an optional nameplate.

p: ROLE: You are a period-photography stylist and colorist. Using the attached photo, transport this person to the year [DECADE]. Keep the exact same face, identity and recognizable features, but restyle the hair, makeup and wardrobe to be authentically of that era, and place them in this setting: [SCENE]. THINK FIRST (silently): pick the era-correct film look before editing. Match the photographic look of the period: for the 70s/80s use warm grainy film with a slight color shift and soft focus; for the 90s use a flash-lit point-and-shoot snapshot look; for Y2K/early 2000s use a harsh on-camera flash, slight overexposure and digital-camera color. Add the appropriate grain, contrast and color cast so it reads as a real photo from that decade, not a clean modern edit. CONSTRAINTS: do not change face shape or make a different person, photoreal, era-correct props only, accurate hands, no modern phones or logos unless the decade allows. OUTPUT: the edited image only.
h: Upload a clear photo, then choose the [DECADE] (e.g. 1985) and an optional [SCENE] (e.g. mall arcade).

p: ROLE: You are a film-emulation specialist replicating a 2000s point-and-shoot. Using the attached photo, restyle it to look like an authentic early-2000s disposable-camera snapshot. Keep the same people, poses and setting recognizable. Apply: harsh direct on-camera flash with bright foreground and falloff into shadow, slight red-eye feel, visible film grain, mild motion blur, lowered dynamic range, a faint warm-green color cast and tiny dust specks. Add a thin white film border and, in the lower-right corner, a small orange-yellow digital date stamp reading exactly "[TIMESTAMP]". Optionally handwrite "[CAPTION]" in the white bottom border. Goal: a deliberately imperfect, nostalgic 'found photo' feel that fights the over-clean AI look. CONSTRAINTS: keep faces recognizable, do NOT beautify or smooth skin, no high-dynamic-range polish, legible date stamp, keep it grounded and real. OUTPUT: the edited image only.
h: Upload a photo, then set an optional [TIMESTAMP] (e.g. 06 22 2006) and a [CAPTION] for the white border.

p: ROLE: You are a precision retoucher doing a non-destructive local edit. Using the attached photo, make exactly ONE targeted change and nothing else: [ONE CHANGE]. Everything else must stay identical: [WHAT TO KEEP] — specifically keep the same face, expression, head pose, body position, framing, camera angle, lighting direction and color temperature, and keep the background unless the change explicitly involves it. THINK FIRST (silently): decide which pixels must move and which must be left untouched. Blend the edit so it matches the existing shadows, reflections and grain of the original, with realistic fabric folds, edges and contact shadows. Do not restyle the whole image, do not shift the crop, and do not subtly alter the person's identity. CONSTRAINTS: photoreal, seamless edge blending, preserve original resolution and detail, no added people or objects beyond the requested change. OUTPUT: the edited image only.
h: Upload a photo, then state the [ONE CHANGE] you want and the [WHAT TO KEEP] you must not alter.

p: ROLE: You are a character-consistency artist building a reusable storyboard character. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): where to place the text block so it never covers a face, which word gets the biggest weight, and the single bold contrast device (thick stroke, glow, or color block) that keeps text readable over any background. Then write ONE Ideogram prompt specifying: a punchy 16:9 composition with the hook text occupying one third of the frame in an extra-bold condensed sans with a heavy outline or color-block backing, an open area reserved for a person or subject, high-saturation contrast, dramatic lighting matched to [EMOTION], and the accent color (or a high-contrast default) as explicit hex. Append parameters on the same line: 'Style: Design, Aspect ratio: 16:9, Magic Prompt: Off'. RULES: render the hook EXACTLY with correct casing; no extra micro-text or gibberish labels; keep the text to a few words so it stays sharp at small sizes; no watermark. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) PROMPT: <one copy-paste line, hook in double quotes, params appended> 2) FACE ZONE: <one line on where to leave space for the subject> 3) CTR TIP: <one line on the contrast device used>. Output nothing else.
h: Set the [HOOK TEXT] (2-4 words), the [TOPIC], an [EMOTION] (shock, hype, curiosity), and an optional [ACCENT COLOR].

p: Act as a senior brand identity designer using Ideogram V3 for typographic logo concepts where the lettering must be perfect. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): what association the mark must trigger, whether a pure wordmark or a lettermark plus a small icon reads best, and which letterforms to feature. Then write ONE Ideogram prompt that specifies: a centered wordmark logo on a clean solid background, a named type personality (geometric sans, humanist serif, monospaced techy, etc.), precise letter spacing, an optional small symbol that does NOT replace any letters, the tagline in smaller tracked caps beneath, a 2-color palette as explicit hex codes, and a flat vector feel with no gradients and no photorealism. Append parameters on the same line: 'Style: Design, Aspect ratio: 1:1, Magic Prompt: Off'. RULES: spell the brand name and tagline letter-for-letter with no extra characters; no fake registration symbols unless asked; no busy texture; the mark must still work in one flat color. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) PROMPT: <one copy-paste line, exact text in double quotes, params appended> 2) PALETTE: <two hex codes + roles> 3) SCALABILITY NOTE: <one line on keeping the name legible when shrunk>. Output nothing else.
h: Give [BRAND NAME], [WHAT IT DOES], an optional [TAGLINE], and a [STYLE] (modern, vintage, techy, playful).

Style: [POSTER STYLE] (e.g. 70s screenprint, brutalist type, neon synthwave, swiss minimalist). THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): the visual hierarchy order (title biggest, then subline, then date/venue), where to place each block, and which display font matches the era. Then write ONE Ideogram prompt specifying: a poster composition with clear typographic hierarchy and breathing room, the named font/era feel, exact casing for each line, a 2-3 color palette as explicit hex codes true to the style, and a simple supporting graphic that never overlaps the text. Append parameters on the same line: 'Style: Design, Aspect ratio: 2:3, Magic Prompt: Off'. RULES: every string spelled exactly with correct dates and punctuation; no duplicated or garbled text; no random extra words; high legibility. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) PROMPT: <one copy-paste line, all three strings in double quotes, params appended> 2) HIERARCHY: <order of text sizes> 3) PALETTE: <hex codes + roles>. Output nothing else.
h: Set [EVENT TITLE], the [DATE & VENUE] line, an optional [SUBLINE], and a [POSTER STYLE].

p: You are a packaging designer using Ideogram V3 to mock up front-of-pack typography that reads perfectly. Product type: [PRODUCT TYPE]. Then write ONE Ideogram prompt specifying: a clean studio product shot of the [PRODUCT TYPE] with a printed label, the three strings placed in clear hierarchy with correct spelling and casing, a named type style, a 2-3 color palette as explicit hex codes, soft front lighting, and a neutral seamless background. Append parameters on the same line: 'Style: Realistic, Aspect ratio: 4:5, Magic Prompt: Off' (Realistic for the product, Magic Prompt off so label text stays literal). RULES: spell every string exactly; keep claim text short and legible; no gibberish ingredient lists or fake fine print; no warped label; no second product. This is a design concept only, not a real health, nutrition or safety claim - keep wording generic. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) PROMPT: <one copy-paste line, the three strings in double quotes, params appended> 2) PALETTE: <hex codes + roles> 3) LEGIBILITY TIP: <one line on keeping small label text crisp>. Output nothing else.
h: Give [BRAND], the [PRODUCT NAME], a [KEY CLAIM] line, and the [PRODUCT TYPE] (box, pouch, can, jar, bottle).

OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) PROMPT: <one copy-paste line, exact phrase in double quotes, params appended> 2) LINE BREAKS: <show how the phrase wraps> 3) PALETTE: <hex codes + roles>. Output nothing else.
h: Put your phrase in [PHRASE], pick an [AESTHETIC] (bauhaus, art-deco, grunge, pastel), and an [ORIENTATION].

p: You are an editor who writes natural-language image-edit prompts for text-capable models (Ideogram remix/edit) that render legible text on an EXISTING uploaded photo. Output nothing else.

p: You are an editorial information designer who writes image-generation prompts for clean, print-grade infographics. Build ONE single-stat hero graphic. Statistic: [STAT]. p: You are a senior infographic art director who writes prompts for tall, magazine-style vertical infographics. Build ONE long-form vertical infographic. Topic: [TOPIC]. Ordered sections (each a heading and what it should show, such as a stat, a small chart, or an icon row): [SECTIONS]. Palette: [PALETTE]. THINK FIRST (silently): how to give the whole piece one visual rhythm, where to vary block types so it does not feel repetitive, and how a reader's eye flows top to bottom. Then write ONE copy-paste image prompt describing: a tall narrow canvas with a bold title header at the top, a stack of clearly separated content sections in the given order, each section using a different but complementary block treatment (a hero stat, a mini bar or donut, an icon row, a short numbered list) suited to its content, consistent section headings, alternating soft background bands to separate sections, a coherent 3-4 color palette named as hex codes reused throughout, a footer with space for a source and credit line, flat vector style, unified type scale, generous spacing. HARD RULES: one section per item given and in that exact order, headings spelled exactly as provided, consistent palette and type across all sections, no clashing art styles, no chart junk, keep it readable when scaled down. End with parameters: --ar 9:16 --v 6 --style raw. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) PROMPT: <one line> 2) SECTION PLAN: <each section heading with the block type you chose for it>. Nothing else.
h: Give the overall topic in [TOPIC], the ordered sections in [SECTIONS], optional [PALETTE].

p:
You are an architectural photographer who shoots for premium real-estate listings. Build me one image of a [ROOM TYPE] decorated in [DESIGN STYLE], during [TIME OF DAY].

Think first (one line, internal): what wide-but-honest angle makes the space feel large without distorting it, and where the natural light enters.

Hard constraints:
- Eye-level camera at roughly chest height, 24mm wide-angle lens, two-point perspective so all vertical lines stay perfectly straight (no fisheye bulge).
- Soft, even daylight from a clear window; gentle highlights, open shadows, no harsh hotspots or lens flare.
- Styled but realistic: tidy and lived-in, nothing cluttered, no people, no text, no brand logos, no warped furniture or invented doorways.
- Neutral-accurate white balance, true-to-life color, crisp focus front to back.

OUTPUT FORMAT: render the single photo at 3:2 landscape, then append exactly one Midjourney line: --ar 3:2 --style raw --stylize 150 --v 6.1
h: Swap in your room type, design style, and the time of day you want.

p:
You are a virtual home stager. Take the empty room I describe and furnish it convincingly: [EMPTY ROOM DESCRIPTION], styled in [DESIGN STYLE], to appeal to [TARGET BUYER].

Think first (internal): which 4-6 furniture pieces define this room's purpose, and a layout that keeps walkways clear.

Hard constraints:
- Keep the room's existing architecture exactly as described: same windows, doors, flooring, and ceiling height. Add only furniture, rugs, lighting, art, and plants.
- Furniture must sit flat on the floor with correct scale and grounded contact shadows; nothing floating or clipping into walls.
- Cohesive palette of 3-4 colors; balanced, not over-decorated.
- No people, no pets, no text overlays, no fictional structural changes.

OUTPUT FORMAT: render one photoreal interior at 3:2 landscape, then list (a) the furniture placed and (b) the exact color palette as hex codes. End with a Midjourney line: --ar 3:2 --style raw --stylize 200 --v 6.1
h: Describe the empty room, the look you want, and who it should appeal to.

p:
You are an interior designer pitching a makeover. Reimagine this room: current state is [CURRENT ROOM], transformed into [TARGET STYLE], at a [BUDGET LEVEL] budget feel.

Think first (internal): the three highest-impact changes (paint, key furniture, lighting) that read instantly as 'after'.

Hard constraints:
- Keep the room's footprint, window positions, and door locations identical to the current state. Change finishes, furniture, textiles, lighting, and decor only.
- Make the upgrade believable for the stated budget: a thrifty makeover leans on paint, textiles, and rearrangement; a high-end one allows custom built-ins and statement pieces.
- Realistic materials and light, accurate scale, no people, no text, no logos.

OUTPUT FORMAT: render one 'after' photo at 4:5 portrait, then give a 4-line shopping list (paint color name, two furniture items, one lighting fixture) a real person could actually buy. End with: --ar 4:5 --style raw --stylize 180 --v 6.1
h: Describe the current room, your dream style, and a budget feel (high-end vs. thrifty).

p:
You are an architectural visualization artist producing a hero exterior render. Create one twilight 'blue hour' shot of a [BUILDING TYPE] in [ARCHITECTURAL STYLE], set in [SURROUNDING LANDSCAPE].

Think first (internal): the three-quarter angle that shows two facades at once and reveals the building's best feature.

Hard constraints:
- Three-quarter front-corner view, 35mm lens, camera slightly below eye level for a confident stance; verticals kept straight.
- Blue-hour sky (deep indigo to warm amber on the horizon); warm interior lights glowing from windows; subtle landscape and path lighting.
- Crisp, modern materials with accurate reflections; clean lawn or hardscape; a few realistic trees. No people, no cars unless they belong, no text, no watermarks.
- Photoreal, not cartoonish; physically plausible light and shadow.

OUTPUT FORMAT: render one image at 16:9 landscape, then append: --ar 16:9 --style raw --stylize 250 --v 6.1
h: Name the building type, architectural style, and surrounding setting.

p:
You are a lifestyle interiors photographer shooting an intimate vignette. Capture [INTERIOR CORNER OR NOOK] with a [MOOD] feeling, in [SEASON].

Think first (internal): the one tactile detail (a throw blanket, steam off a mug, a stack of books) that signals warmth and use.

Hard constraints:
- 50mm lens at eye level, shallow depth of field so the foreground detail is sharp and the background softly blurred.
- Warm, directional window light with gentle falloff; cozy shadows, no flat overhead lighting.
- Layered textures (wool, wood, ceramic, linen); a believable hint of human presence but no people in frame.
- No text, no logos, no AI-glossy plastic sheen; keep it natural and slightly imperfect.

OUTPUT FORMAT: render one image at 4:5 portrait, then append: --ar 4:5 --style raw --stylize 220 --v 6.1
h: Pick the corner or nook, the mood, and the season outside the window.

p:
You are an architect rendering a presentation floor plan. Produce a top-down, furnished plan view of a [SPACE TYPE] of roughly [SIZE], containing [ROOMS / ZONES].

Think first (internal): a logical adjacency (kitchen near dining, bath near bedrooms) so the flow reads sensibly.

Hard constraints:
- Strict orthographic top-down view (true plan, no perspective tilt).
- Walls shown as clean filled lines; doors with swing arcs; windows marked in walls; furniture drawn to scale from above.
- Soft, designer-magazine color fills per zone; light wood floors, subtle rug textures, small labeled room names.
- Balanced, realistic proportions; clear circulation paths; no people, no clutter, no decorative noise.

OUTPUT FORMAT: render one plan at 1:1 square, then list each room with a one-word color label. End with: --ar 1:1 --stylize 120 --v 6.1
h: Give the space type, square footage or rough size, and the rooms you want.

p:
You are an interior designer assembling a client mood board. Lay out a flat-lay material board for a [ROOM TYPE] in [DESIGN STYLE], anchored by an accent color of [ACCENT COLOR].

Think first (internal): a palette of one accent, two neutrals, plus wood and metal tones that won't clash.

Hard constraints:
- Overhead flat-lay on a clean neutral surface, soft even daylight, gentle natural shadows.
- Include arranged swatches: a paint chip, two fabric squares, a flooring sample, a stone or tile tile, one metal finish, and one small hardware piece.
- Tidy, slightly overlapping arrangement like a real designer's desk; cohesive within the chosen palette.
- No text labels rendered in the image, no logos, no people, no busy background.

OUTPUT FORMAT: render one flat-lay at 1:1 square, then write the palette as named swatches with hex codes below the image. End with: --ar 1:1 --style raw --stylize 160 --v 6.1
h: Name the room, the target style, and one accent color to anchor it.

p:
You are a small-space specialist designer. Show a clever, airy layout for [SMALL SPACE TYPE] measuring [DIMENSIONS], that must fit [REQUIRED FUNCTIONS].

Think first (internal): two or three space-saving moves (vertical storage, multi-use furniture, light palette) that buy the most breathing room.

Hard constraints:
- Eye-level 28mm view from a corner, verticals straight, perspective honest (no fake-spacious distortion).
- Light, reflective palette and a large mirror or window to expand the sense of space; multi-functional furniture (e.g., sofa bed, fold-down desk, under-bench storage).
- Everything scaled to the stated dimensions; clear walking paths; uncluttered but warm.
- No people, no text, no logos, no impossible furniture sizes.

OUTPUT FORMAT: render one image at 3:2 landscape, then list the 3 space-saving tricks used. End with: --ar 3:2 --style raw --stylize 170 --v 6.1
h: State the small space, its real dimensions, and the must-have functions.

p:
You are a lighting designer demonstrating ambience. Render a [ROOM TYPE] at night where a [LIGHT FIXTURE TYPE] sets a [MOOD] atmosphere.

Think first (internal): the layered lighting plan (ambient, task, accent) so the room glows rather than looks dim or blown out.

Hard constraints:
- Eye-level 35mm view; warm color temperature around 2700K from the fixtures; visible pools of light and soft shadow gradients.
- The statement fixture is the clear focal point, correctly scaled to the room and ceiling height; secondary lamps or sconces fill the corners.
- Realistic light physics: gentle bloom, no neon glow, no overexposed hotspots, accurate reflections on surfaces.
- No people, no text, no logos.

OUTPUT FORMAT: render one image at 16:9 landscape, then name the three light layers used (ambient / task / accent). End with: --ar 16:9 --style raw --stylize 200 --v 6.1
h: Choose the room, the fixture type, and the evening mood you want.

p:
You are a landscape and outdoor-living designer. Create an inviting [OUTDOOR SPACE TYPE] in [DESIGN STYLE], suited to a [CLIMATE / REGION].

Think first (internal): the zones (lounge, dining, greenery) and plants that genuinely thrive in the stated climate.

Hard constraints:
- Eye-level 35mm view at golden hour; warm low sun, long soft shadows, natural color.
- Climate-appropriate planting and materials (e.g., drought-tolerant for arid, lush foliage for tropical); comfortable seating, a defined dining or lounge zone, and ground material (deck, stone, gravel) that fits the look.
- Believable scale and depth; a sense of enclosure without feeling cramped; no people, no text, no logos.
- Photoreal greenery, accurate shadows, no plastic-looking plants.

OUTPUT FORMAT: render one image at 3:2 landscape, then list 4 climate-appropriate plants used. End with: --ar 3:2 --style raw --stylize 220 --v 6.1
h: Describe the outdoor space, the style, and the climate or region.

p:
You are a commercial interior designer. Design the interior of a [BUSINESS TYPE] with a [BRAND VIBE], built around the experience of [KEY CUSTOMER MOMENT].

Think first (internal): the focal zone customers see first (counter, display, lounge) and how the layout guides their path.

Hard constraints:
- Eye-level 24mm wide view from the entrance, verticals straight, honest perspective.
- Cohesive brand palette and materials that match the vibe; clear functional zones (service, seating, display) with realistic commercial-grade furniture and signage placement (signage blank, no readable text).
- Daylight plus designed interior lighting; believable foot-traffic flow; clean but characterful, not sterile.
- No people in frame, no readable text or real brand logos, no warped fixtures.

OUTPUT FORMAT: render one image at 16:9 landscape, then describe the customer's path in 3 short steps. End with: --ar 16:9 --style raw --stylize 200 --v 6.1
h: Name the business type, the brand vibe, and the key customer experience.

p:
You are an interior designer focused on a single feature wall. Show a [ROOM TYPE] where one wall becomes a statement using [WALL TREATMENT], within an overall [ROOM STYLE].

Think first (internal): how the rest of the room stays calm so the feature wall reads as the hero, not chaos.

Hard constraints:
- Eye-level 35mm view angled to show the full feature wall plus part of an adjacent wall for context.
- The wall treatment is the clear focal point (e.g., textured plaster, wood slats, bold paint, tile, paneling, or floating shelves), executed cleanly with realistic material depth and shadow.
- Surrounding decor is restrained and complementary; balanced styling; soft natural light grazing the wall to show texture.
- No people, no text, no logos, no clashing colors.

OUTPUT FORMAT: render one image at 4:5 portrait, then give a 3-line how-to-recreate note (material, finish, accent pairing). End with: --ar 4:5 --style raw --stylize 190 --v 6.1
h: Give the room, the wall treatment idea, and the overall room style.

p:
You are an interior designer helping a client decide between two directions. Render the same [ROOM TYPE] twice in a split composition: the left half in [STYLE A], the right half in [STYLE B], with the room's architecture identical on both sides.

Think first (internal): keep the camera, window, and layout matched so only the styling differs and the comparison is fair.

Hard constraints:
- One image split cleanly down the middle; identical 35mm eye-level viewpoint and identical room shell (same walls, window, floor, ceiling) on both halves.
- Each half fully and authentically committed to its style in furniture, palette, textiles, and decor; a thin neutral divider line between them.
- Consistent realistic daylight across both halves; no people, no text labels, no logos.
- Honest, buildable looks, not exaggerated caricatures.

OUTPUT FORMAT: render one split image at 16:9 landscape, then write a 2-line verdict: who each side suits best. End with: --ar 16:9 --style raw --stylize 180 --v 6.1
h: Give the room, then the two styles you're torn between.

p: You are a senior brand-identity designer who writes image-model prompts. Brand: [BRAND] - [WHAT IT DOES]. Desired vibe: [VIBE]. Color steer / things to avoid: [COLOR OR AVOID]. THINK FIRST (silently): the single association the mark must trigger, and which shapes/letterforms/colors earn it; reject cliche symbols (globe, swoosh, lightbulb) unless truly apt. Produce FIVE distinct logo routes: (A) clean wordmark, (B) lettermark/monogram, (C) abstract geometric symbol, (D) emblem/badge, (E) symbol+wordmark combo. For EACH route write ONE copy-paste prompt that specifies: flat vector logo, minimal, geometric construction, a named 2-3 colour palette WITH hex codes, even negative space, solid white background, centered, generous margin, scalable, no gradients, no photorealism, no 3D, no mockup, no text artifacts. OUTPUT FORMAT (Markdown table, columns: Route | Prompt | Midjourney params | Why it fits). Use these MJ params per route: '--ar 1:1 --style raw --no photo, realistic shadow, 3d, gradient'. After the table add: PICK: one line naming the strongest route for [VIBE] and why. Nothing else.
h: Give [BRAND] + [WHAT IT DOES], a [VIBE] (e.g. premium fintech, playful pet brand), and an optional [COLOR/AVOID].

p: Act as a logotype and monogram specialist. Build ONE master image prompt for a geometric monogram using the initials [INITIALS] for a [INDUSTRY] brand, in a [STYLE] direction. THINK FIRST (silently): how the letters can interlock, share a stroke, or create a hidden shape in the negative space; ensure the letters stay READABLE at favicon size. The prompt must specify: single flat vector monogram, built on a visible-then-removed geometric grid (golden ratio or modular), consistent stroke weight, balanced counters, elegant negative space, ONE accent colour on white (give a hex pair), no gradient, no bevel, no drop shadow, no 3D, centered with margin, transparent-ready. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) PROMPT: <one copy-paste line>. 2) MIDJOURNEY VERSION: same line ending '--ar 1:1 --style raw --s 80 --no shadow, gradient, 3d, photo'. 3) IDEOGRAM/DALL-E NOTE: <one line on rendering the letters cleanly>. 4) READABILITY CHECK: <one line on how to confirm it works at 32px>. Nothing else.
h: Set [INITIALS] (1-3 letters), the [INDUSTRY], and a [STYLE] (e.g. luxury serif, techy sans, hand-drawn).

p: You are a mascot and character-logo designer. Brand: [BRAND + AUDIENCE]. Base character: [ANIMAL OR CHARACTER TYPE]. Personality: [PERSONALITY]. THINK FIRST (silently): one memorable silhouette and one signature feature that makes it instantly ownable and trademark-distinct; keep it simple enough to reproduce as a flat logo. Deliver in this OUTPUT FORMAT: 1) LOCKED IDENTITY BLOCK: a plain-text line fixing species/type, body proportions (chibi vs sleek), exact colour palette with hex codes, signature accessory, and expression - this stays byte-for-byte identical across renders. 2) MASTER LOGO PROMPT: flat vector mascot, bold clean outlines, limited palette, friendly expression, solid white background, centered, scalable, no gradient mesh, no photoreal fur, no 3D render; append '--ar 1:1 --style raw --no photo, 3d, realistic'. 3) THREE POSE PROMPTS: waving, thinking, celebrating - each PREPENDS the identity block verbatim so the mascot looks identical. 4) CONSISTENCY NOTE: one line on using --cref / a reference image to lock the face. Nothing else.
h: Describe the [BRAND + AUDIENCE], pick an [ANIMAL/CHARACTER TYPE], and a [PERSONALITY] (e.g. cheeky, wise, energetic).

p: You are an app-icon designer who knows iOS/Android icon grids and the 'one idea, one shape' rule. App: [APP NAME + WHAT IT DOES]. Core metaphor to explore: [CORE METAPHOR]. Platform: [PLATFORM]. THINK FIRST (silently): reduce the brand to ONE bold glyph centered on the icon keyline grid; ensure it reads as a 1-bit silhouette and survives at 16px; avoid tiny details, text, and thin lines. The prompt must specify: single app icon, centered bold glyph, flat or subtle single-direction gradient duotone, rounded-square safe area with padding, vibrant 2-colour palette WITH hex, crisp edges, solid background, no text, no photoreal, no skeuomorphic clutter, no drop shadow. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) ICON PROMPT: <one copy-paste line>. 2) MIDJOURNEY: same line + '--ar 1:1 --style raw --s 120 --no text, photo, 3d clutter'. 3) SCALE TEST: <one line on checking it at 16/32/180px>. 4) LIGHT/DARK NOTE: <one line on a background swap for both modes>. Nothing else.
h: Give [APP NAME + WHAT IT DOES], a [CORE METAPHOR] you like, and the [PLATFORM] (iOS, Android, web).

p: Act as a brand strategist + art director shipping a coherent starter identity. Brand: [BRAND + WHAT IT DOES]. Customer: [TARGET CUSTOMER]. Positioning: [POSITIONING]. THINK FIRST (silently): the ONE strategic idea every visual choice must ladder up to, and reject any element that does not reinforce it. OUTPUT FORMAT - return these labelled sections only: 1) BRAND IDEA: one sentence. 2) PALETTE: primary, secondary, neutral, accent - each with name, exact hex, and a use note; confirm body text vs background passes WCAG AA contrast. 3) TYPOGRAPHY: a display + body pairing (Google Fonts only) with a one-line rationale and fallback web-safe stack. 4) LOGO CONCEPT: 2-3 sentence direction naming the mark type (wordmark / symbol+wordmark / monogram). 5) MOTIF/PATTERN: one repeatable graphic device derived from the logo. 6) BRAND VOICE: 3 adjectives + one do/don't line. 7) IMAGE PROMPTS: (a) a flat-vector LOGO prompt and (b) a seamless brand-PATTERN prompt - both specifying flat vector, the exact palette hexes, solid white background, even negative space, no gradient, no photoreal, no 3D, append '--ar 1:1 --style raw'. Reuse identical hex values across every section - no drift. Nothing else.
h: Describe [BRAND + WHAT IT DOES], the [TARGET CUSTOMER], and a [POSITIONING] (e.g. premium-minimal, bold-disruptor).

p: You are a logo designer famous for negative-space and dual-meaning marks. Brand: [BRAND + WHAT IT DOES]. The visible shape should be [SHAPE A]; the hidden shape revealed in the negative space should be [SHAPE B]. THINK FIRST (silently): how the gap between elements forms [SHAPE B] WITHOUT being forced; both readings must be clean and instantly legible in a single colour. The prompt must specify: minimalist flat vector logo, clever negative-space concept where the empty space forms [SHAPE B], single solid colour (give a hex), perfectly balanced, geometric, solid white background, centered, no gradient, no shadow, no 3D, no text, scalable to one colour. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) CONCEPT: one line explaining the hidden idea. 2) PROMPT: <one copy-paste line>. 3) MIDJOURNEY: same line + '--ar 1:1 --style raw --s 60 --no gradient, shadow, 3d, text, photo'. 4) FALLBACK: one line on how to simplify if the hidden shape reads as noise. Nothing else.
h: Give [BRAND + WHAT IT DOES] and two concepts to fuse: [SHAPE A] hiding [SHAPE B] in the negative space.

p: Act as a personal-branding designer. Person: [FULL NAME], known for [NICHE]. Desired feel: [FEEL]. THINK FIRST (silently): which of three formats suits this person best - (a) initials monogram, (b) stylised signature, (c) clean name wordmark - and pick the strongest, but provide all three. For EACH format write ONE image prompt specifying: flat vector personal logo, refined letterforms, balanced spacing, single accent colour on white WITH a hex pair, minimal, no gradient, no photoreal, no 3D, centered with margin, scalable to a profile-pic circle. OUTPUT FORMAT (Markdown table: Format | Prompt | Midjourney params | Best used for). MJ params: '--ar 1:1 --style raw --no shadow, 3d, photo, busy detail'. After the table: RECOMMEND one format for [NICHE] in a single line, and add a PFP TIP line on cropping it into a circular avatar. Nothing else.
h: Give [FULL NAME], what you're known for in [NICHE], and a [FEEL] (e.g. editorial, techy, handcrafted).

p: You are a no-nonsense art director reviewing a logo. Logo: [DESCRIPTION OR UPLOAD]. Brand context: [BRAND CONTEXT]. Where it feels off: [WHAT FEELS WRONG]. Be brutally honest and specific - do not flatter. THINK FIRST (silently): judge it against scalability, legibility at small size, uniqueness, balance, colour, timelessness, and concept. OUTPUT FORMAT - return: 1) SCORECARD: a table scoring each of those 7 criteria /10 with a one-line reason. 2) TOP 3 PROBLEMS: ranked, each with the fix. 3) KEEP: what is actually working (be fair). 4) REDESIGN BRIEF: a tight paragraph an image model can use. 5) FIXED PROMPT: one copy-paste flat-vector prompt implementing your advice, with hex palette, white background, no gradient/3d/photo, ending '--ar 1:1 --style raw'. Note: this is design feedback, not a legal trademark clearance - tell me to run a separate trademark search before using any mark commercially. Nothing else.
h: Paste a [DESCRIPTION OR UPLOAD] of the logo, the [BRAND CONTEXT], and where it [FAILS/FEELS OFF].

p: You are a merch and sticker designer. Brand: [BRAND + VIBE]. Palette: [PALETTE OR HEX]. Motifs to include: [MOTIFS]. THINK FIRST (silently): how to keep 4-6 small designs visually unified by colour, line weight, and a thick white die-cut border, while each works alone. The prompt must specify: a cohesive sheet of die-cut vinyl stickers, flat bold vector style, thick white kiss-cut outline around each, consistent limited palette WITH hex, clean shapes, no fine gradients, no photoreal, on a neutral flat-lay background, even lighting, top-down. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) STICKER LIST: 4-6 sticker ideas in one line each. 2) SHEET PROMPT: <one copy-paste line for the full sheet>. 3) SINGLE-STICKER PROMPT: <one line for the hero sticker alone>. 4) MIDJOURNEY: append '--ar 4:5 --style raw --no busy gradient, photo realism, thin lines'. 5) PRINT TIP: one line on bleed/safe-zone for print-on-demand. Nothing else.
h: Give [BRAND + VIBE], your [PALETTE/HEX], and 3-5 [MOTIFS] you want on the stickers.

p: Act as a brand-systems designer producing a logo usage page for a style guide. Logo: [LOGO - symbol + name]. Brand colours: [BRAND COLORS OR HEX]. Primary use: [PRIMARY USE]. THINK FIRST (silently): which lockups this brand actually needs for [PRIMARY USE], and the minimum-size + clear-space rules that protect the mark from crowding and distortion. OUTPUT FORMAT - return labelled sections only: 1) LOCKUPS: define horizontal, stacked/vertical, icon-only, and one-colour (mono) versions, each in one sentence with its intended placement. 2) CLEAR SPACE: a rule expressed in units of the mark (e.g. 'minimum padding = the height of the symbol on all sides'). 3) MIN SIZE: smallest legible pixel/mm size for icon-only vs full lockup, for screen and print. 4) COLOUR VARIANTS: full-colour, all-black, all-white (knockout/reversed) - each with exact hex and the background it sits on. 5) MISUSE: 5 do-not rules (no stretch/distort, no recolour, no drop shadow, no busy/low-contrast background, no rotation). 6) IMAGE PROMPT: one flat-vector prompt that lays out all lockups and the clear-space diagram on a clean labelled grid board, solid white background, no gradient/3d/photo, ending '--ar 16:9 --style raw'. Nothing else.
h: Describe your [LOGO] (symbol + name), the [BRAND COLORS/HEX], and where it'll appear most: [PRIMARY USE].

p: You are an esports logo illustrator. Team/channel: [TEAM OR CHANNEL NAME]. Mascot: [MASCOT]. Vibe: [VIBE]. THINK FIRST (silently): one fierce, recognizable mascot head or crest with a strong silhouette that reads as a tiny circular avatar AND scales onto a jersey; avoid muddy detail. The prompt must specify: bold esports mascot logo, aggressive dynamic angle, thick clean black outlines, cel-shaded with sharp highlights, vivid 3-colour palette WITH hex, optional banner for the name, solid contrasting background, centered, high contrast, vector-style, no photoreal, no soft blur, no messy gradients. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) PROMPT: <one copy-paste line>. 2) MIDJOURNEY: same line + '--ar 1:1 --style raw --s 250 --no blur, photo, soft gradient, low contrast'. 3) AVATAR CROP: one line on the circular Twitch/Discord crop. 4) MONO VERSION: one line on a single-colour jersey-embroidery variant. Nothing else.
h: Give [TEAM/CHANNEL NAME], a [MASCOT] (animal/creature), and a [VIBE] (e.g. neon-cyber, gritty, regal).

p: You are an editing-focused image-model operator (Nano Banana / FLUX Kontext style). Base logo: [CURRENT LOGO]. Themes to generate: [NEW THEMES]. THINK FIRST (silently): the surgical-edit rule - change ONLY the requested theme element and explicitly preserve the mark's shape, proportions, letterforms, and composition. For EACH theme write one conversational edit prompt in this pattern: 'Restyle this logo as [THEME] - change [colour/texture/ornament] while keeping the exact same shape, proportions, letterforms, and layout; keep it flat vector and legible at small size.' Quote any literal text to keep with double quotes. OUTPUT FORMAT (Markdown table: Theme | Edit prompt | What to preserve | What changes). Add a final row 'Midjourney/MJ note' explaining to use '--sref [logo url] --sw 150 --ar 1:1 --style raw' instead, since MJ can't edit-in-place. Then one CAUTION line: keep edits subtle so brand recognition is preserved. Nothing else.
h: Upload/describe the [CURRENT LOGO], then list the [NEW THEMES] you want (e.g. holiday, monochrome, retro).

p: Act as a brand naming strategist who thinks in pictures. Product: [WHAT YOU SELL]. Audience + vibe: [AUDIENCE + VIBE]. Avoid: [MUST-AVOID]. THINK FIRST (silently): generate names that are short, pronounceable, .com-plausible, and ALREADY suggest a visual mark (a hidden object, a letter quirk, a metaphor). OUTPUT FORMAT - a Markdown table with columns: Name | Type (coined / compound / real-word / metaphor) | Why it works | Built-in logo idea | Logo image prompt. Give 8 names. Each 'Logo image prompt' must be a flat-vector, single copy-paste line with a hex palette, white background, no gradient/3d/photo, ending '--ar 1:1 --style raw'. After the table: TOP PICK - name the strongest name+mark pairing for [AUDIENCE + VIBE] and why, in one line. Add a NOTE: I have not checked trademark or domain availability - run a separate trademark and domain search before committing. Nothing else.
h: Describe [WHAT YOU SELL], the [AUDIENCE + VIBE], and any [MUST-AVOID] words or rivals.

p: You are a fashion-magazine cinematographer who writes Midjourney v6/v7 prompts. Turn my idea into ONE photoreal editorial beauty portrait. Person: [PERSON]. Mood: [MOOD]. Palette: [PALETTE]. THINK FIRST (silently, never shown): where the key light sits, what lens flatters the face, what single emotion the frame holds, what kills the 'too-perfect AI' look. Then write ONE copy-paste line of cinematic, comma-separated descriptors in this order: subject + expression, wardrobe + styling, environment, lighting (name the source + color temperature in K, e.g. soft Rembrandt key 5600K), lens + camera (e.g. 85mm f/1.4, shallow depth of field), color grade, skin and detail cues (visible pores, fine flyaway hairs, natural asymmetry), mood. HARD RULES: keep real skin texture, no waxy smoothing, no extra fingers, no text, no logos. End the line with realistic params: --ar 4:5 --v 6 --style raw --s 250. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) PROMPT: <one line> 2) TWO TWEAKS: <two single-word swaps that shift the mood>. Nothing else.
h: Describe the [PERSON] in plain terms; set a [MOOD] and an optional [PALETTE].
