h: Attach or describe the [COVER ARTWORK], pick the [FORMAT] (e.g. standing hardcover, held paperback, open magazine spread), and optionally set [PAPER FINISH] and [BACKDROP].

p: ROLE: You are a merchandise photographer who shoots branded everyday goods. TASK: Apply the supplied [DESIGN/LOGO] to a [PRODUCT] so it reads as a real, manufactured merch item. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide how the design conforms to this product's surface — embroidery raised on a cap's curved front, glaze-print wrapping a mug's cylinder, screen-print sitting flat on a tote's weave — plus the angle and setting. THEN GENERATE: render the [PRODUCT COLOR] [PRODUCT] with the design applied in the correct technique for its material: embroidery with visible thread texture on a cap, a print that curves around a mug and foreshortens toward its sides, or ink that sinks into canvas weave on a tote; place it in [SETTING] with a softly blurred background; light with a soft key, a clean edge highlight, accurate material texture (fabric weave, ceramic sheen, stitch relief) and a grounded shadow. CONSTRAINTS: keep the design's colors, scale and text exactly as supplied — conformed to the product's curve, stitch or weave, never a flat floating sticker; realistic material and finish; legible, undistorted artwork; one product, no extra logos or added text. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished merch mockup only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the product, color or setting.
h: Attach or describe the [DESIGN/LOGO], name the [PRODUCT] (e.g. embroidered cap, ceramic mug, canvas tote), and optionally set [PRODUCT COLOR] and [SETTING].

p: ROLE: You are a packaging-experience photographer who shoots DTC unboxing scenes. TASK: Stage the supplied [PRODUCT] inside an open branded box mid-reveal in a [UNBOXING STYLE]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the overhead-or-three-quarter angle, how the lid, tissue and insert frame the product, and the warm light that makes it feel like a real first moment. THEN GENERATE: render an open box with the lid lifted or set aside, the [PRODUCT] nestled in protective fill appropriate to the [UNBOXING STYLE] (crisp tissue and a thank-you card for luxury, kraft shred for eco, a tray of items for a gift set); apply any supplied [BOX/INSERT ARTWORK] accurately to the box interior, lid or card following each surface; place it on a [SURFACE] with a [MOOD]; light from above with a soft, warm key, gentle shadows and tactile material texture (paper, tissue, cardboard, the product's own finish). CONSTRAINTS: keep the product and any supplied artwork exactly as given — artwork printed onto the box/card surfaces, not floating; photoreal materials, fill and shadows; a believable, uncluttered reveal; one hero product, no fake brand text beyond what's supplied. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished unboxing scene only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the unboxing style, surface or mood.
h: Attach or describe the [PRODUCT] and any [BOX/INSERT ARTWORK], pick the [UNBOXING STYLE] (e.g. luxury tissue reveal, eco kraft fill, gift set), and optionally set [SURFACE] and [MOOD].

p: ROLE: You are a signage and environmental-branding visualizer who mocks up storefronts for clients. TASK: Apply the supplied [LOGO/SIGN ARTWORK] to a [STOREFRONT TYPE] at the chosen [SIGN PLACEMENT] within a real street scene. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the street-level viewing angle, how the logo sits on the chosen surface (raised channel letters, a flat window decal, printed awning, or a projecting blade sign), and how daylight and glass reflections behave. THEN GENERATE: build a believable [STOREFRONT TYPE] facade in a [STREET MOOD] scene, with the supplied artwork mounted at [SIGN PLACEMENT] — conformed to that surface's perspective and material (dimensional lettering with subtle depth and shadow, a semi-transparent window decal over glass, or print on fabric/awning); add real-world context (sidewalk, glass with soft reflections, greenery, faint passersby) and natural directional light with grounded shadows; keep the logo crisp, correctly scaled and color-true. CONSTRAINTS: keep the logo/sign artwork exactly as supplied — mounted to the storefront in correct perspective, never flat-pasted or floating; photoreal facade, glass, materials and light; no gibberish signage elsewhere, no warped logo text; one primary sign placement. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished storefront mockup only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 3:2 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the storefront type, sign placement or street mood.
h: Attach or describe the [LOGO/SIGN ARTWORK], pick the [STOREFRONT TYPE] (e.g. cafe, boutique, salon), and optionally set [SIGN PLACEMENT] (sign band, window decal, awning, blade sign) and [STREET MOOD].

p: ROLE: You are a magazine-cover portrait photographer who writes surgically precise image-model prompts. TASK: Using the uploaded photo as the single source of identity, build a hyper-real 85mm editorial HEADSHOT of this exact person, keeping their face, skin tone, hairline, eye color and bone structure unmistakably recognizable. Vibe/role: [VIBE/ROLE]. Wardrobe: [WARDROBE]. Backdrop: [BACKDROP COLOR]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): where the key light sits, what separates real skin from plastic, which crop flatters this face, how to keep identity locked. SKIN + DETAIL CONSTRAINTS: keep visible pores, fine vellus hair, natural specular highlights and subtle subsurface glow; ENFORCE eyes tack-sharp with catchlights; BAN airbrushed/waxy/over-smoothed skin, extra or fused fingers, warped ears, mangled teeth, duplicated faces. OUTPUT FORMAT (print exactly these three labeled blocks, nothing before or after): 1) NANO-BANANA / DALL-E PROMPT: one conversational paragraph naming an 85mm f/1.8 look, a 3-light studio setup (key + soft rim + low fill), catchlights at 10 o'clock, shallow depth of field, and the literal line 'keep the same face and identity, photo-realistic not illustrated'. 2) MIDJOURNEY V7: one line ordered Subject > Wardrobe > Lighting > Lens, ending exactly with --ar 4:5 --style raw --s 120 --hd. 3) FLUX KONTEXT EDIT: one 'change only X, preserve the same facial features, expression and composition' instruction. Output nothing else.
h: Upload a clear front-facing photo, then set the [VIBE/ROLE], an optional [WARDROBE] and a [BACKDROP COLOR].

p: ROLE: You are a commercial product photographer shooting hero frames for a premium e-commerce storefront. TASK: Using the uploaded photo as the exact product reference, create a hyper-real STUDIO HERO SHOT of this product: [PRODUCT]. Surface/mood: [SURFACE/MOOD]. Accent color: [BRAND COLOR]. NON-NEGOTIABLE: keep the product's real shape, material, proportions, logo and label text legible and undistorted. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): how light should wrap this material (glossy vs matte vs glass vs metal), where a soft reflection grounds it, what background makes it pop without distraction. MATERIAL + LIGHT CONSTRAINTS: physically plausible reflections and contact shadow, seamless soft-gradient backdrop, large softbox key from upper-left, gentle gradient reflection on a clean surface; BAN floating products, gibberish or altered label text, harsh blown highlights, warped geometry, duplicated product. OUTPUT FORMAT (print exactly these three labeled blocks, nothing before or after): 1) NANO-BANANA / DALL-E PROMPT: one conversational paragraph describing the studio setup, a 100mm macro lens at f/8 for full sharpness, the lighting and reflection, and the literal line 'preserve the exact product, logo and label text unchanged'. 2) MIDJOURNEY V7: one line ordered Subject > Surface > Lighting > Style, ending exactly with --ar 1:1 --style raw --s 100 --hd. 3) FLUX KONTEXT EDIT: one 'replace the background with a clean studio gradient while keeping the same product, logo, label and lighting' instruction. Output nothing else.
h: Upload your product photo, then name the [PRODUCT], a [SURFACE/MOOD] and an optional [BRAND COLOR].

p: ROLE: You are a natural-light portrait photographer known for warm, filmic outdoor work. TASK: Using the uploaded photo as the single source of identity, build a hyper-real GOLDEN-HOUR portrait of this exact person with identity fully preserved. Location: [LOCATION]. Mood: [TIME-OF-DAY MOOD]. Outfit: [OUTFIT]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): where the low sun sits to create a hair/rim light, how to retain shadow detail on the face, how much haze is allowed before it kills sharpness. LIGHT + SKIN CONSTRAINTS: backlit low-angle sun, warm ~3200K rim on hair and shoulders, soft bounced fill on the face, gentle lens flare, hazy bokeh; keep pores, peach-fuzz and natural skin sheen; eyes tack-sharp; BAN HDR-crunch, orange-teal overcook, plastic skin, double rim lights, identity drift. OUTPUT FORMAT (print exactly these three labeled blocks, nothing before or after): 1) NANO-BANANA / DALL-E PROMPT: one conversational paragraph naming an 85mm f/1.4 lens, golden-hour backlight, sharp eyes, and the literal line 'keep the same face and identity'. 2) MIDJOURNEY V7: one line ordered Subject > Location > Lighting > Lens, ending exactly with --ar 4:5 --style raw --s 150 --hd. 3) FLUX KONTEXT EDIT: one 'relight this portrait to warm golden-hour backlight while preserving the same face, pose and composition' instruction. Output nothing else.
h: Upload the photo, then set the [LOCATION], the [TIME-OF-DAY MOOD] and an optional [OUTFIT].

p: ROLE: You are a corporate headshot photographer who shoots executives and is paid to make people look credible and approachable. TASK: Using the uploaded photo as the single source of identity, create a hyper-real CORPORATE HEADSHOT of this exact person with identity preserved. Field/level: [INDUSTRY/SENIORITY]. Backdrop: [BACKDROP]. Attire: [ATTIRE]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): the exact difference between 'AI-perfect' and 'professionally photographed' - natural micro-asymmetry and real skin texture are what sell trust. LIGHT + REALISM CONSTRAINTS: soft Rembrandt key at 45 degrees, gentle fill, subtle hair light, shallow depth with the background softly blurred, genuine catchlights, relaxed confident expression; keep pores and slight skin texture; BAN over-whitened teeth, waxy skin, a frozen fake smile, distorted glasses frames, symmetrical doll face. OUTPUT FORMAT (print exactly these three labeled blocks, nothing before or after): 1) NANO-BANANA / DALL-E PROMPT: one conversational paragraph naming an 85mm f/2 lens, three-quarter crop at chest level, soft studio light, and the literal line 'keep the same face and identity, photo-realistic not illustrated'. 2) MIDJOURNEY V7: one line ordered Subject > Attire > Lighting > Lens, ending exactly with --ar 4:5 --style raw --s 100 --hd. 3) FLUX KONTEXT EDIT: one 'swap the background to a clean neutral studio backdrop while keeping the same face, expression and lighting' instruction. Output nothing else.
h: Upload your photo, then pick the [INDUSTRY/SENIORITY], a [BACKDROP] and an optional [ATTIRE].

p: ROLE: You are a cinematic portrait photographer specializing in neo-noir. TASK: Using the uploaded photo as the single source of identity, build a hyper-real NEO-NOIR CAR PORTRAIT of this exact person with identity preserved, seated in a car at night beside a rain-streaked window. Neon palette/city: [CITY/NEON PALETTE]. Expression: [EXPRESSION]. Outfit: [OUTFIT]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): where neon reflections fall on skin and glass, how rain droplets refract the light, how to keep the eyes readable in low key. LIGHT + MOOD CONSTRAINTS: moody low-key lighting, colored neon rim and reflections on wet glass, water droplets catching bokeh, a soft practical light on the face, deep shadows that retain detail, shallow depth of field; keep real skin texture; BAN muddy black-crush, plastic skin, duplicate reflections of the face, identity drift. OUTPUT FORMAT (print exactly these three labeled blocks, nothing before or after): 1) NANO-BANANA / DALL-E PROMPT: one conversational paragraph naming an 85mm f/1.4 lens, night neon-noir, rain-streaked window, shallow focus, and the literal line 'keep the same face and identity'. 2) MIDJOURNEY V7: one line ordered Subject > Setting > Neon Lighting > Lens, ending exactly with --ar 16:9 --style raw --s 250 --hd. 3) FLUX KONTEXT EDIT: one 'add neon reflections and rain on the window while keeping the same face, pose and composition' instruction. Output nothing else.
h: Upload the photo, then set the [CITY/NEON PALETTE], an optional [EXPRESSION] and [OUTFIT].

p: ROLE: You are a beauty photographer shooting a cosmetics campaign. TASK: Using the uploaded photo as the single source of identity, build a hyper-real BEAUTY CLOSE-UP of this exact person with identity preserved, framed tight on the face. Look: [MAKEUP/LOOK]. Skin finish: [SKIN FINISH]. Accent color: [ACCENT COLOR]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): real beauty work shows texture, not erasure; where dewy highlights and a ring-light catchlight should sit; how to hold the iris in macro focus. SKIN + DETAIL CONSTRAINTS: visible pores and fine vellus hair, glossy dewy highlights on cheekbones and lips, soft even beauty lighting, crisp eyelashes and brows, clean ring-light or butterfly catchlight, macro sharpness on the iris; BAN airbrushed-to-plastic skin, smeared eyelashes, over-saturated lips, warped facial symmetry. OUTPUT FORMAT (print exactly these three labeled blocks, nothing before or after): 1) NANO-BANANA / DALL-E PROMPT: one conversational paragraph naming a 100mm macro lens, butterfly or ring beauty lighting, tight crop, and the literal line 'keep the same face and identity, real skin texture'. 2) MIDJOURNEY V7: one line ordered Subject > Look > Lighting > Lens, ending exactly with --ar 4:5 --style raw --s 120 --hd. 3) FLUX KONTEXT EDIT: one 'add a dewy beauty finish and brighter catchlights while preserving the exact face and skin texture' instruction. Output nothing else.
h: Upload the photo, then set a [MAKEUP/LOOK], an optional [SKIN FINISH] and [ACCENT COLOR].

p: ROLE: You are a still-life and set-design photographer who styles flat-lays for premium brands. TASK: Using the uploaded photo as the exact product reference, build a hyper-real STYLED PRODUCT SCENE around this product: [PRODUCT]. Theme: [SCENE/THEME]. Props: [PROPS]. NON-NEGOTIABLE: keep the product's real shape, material, color and label text legible and undistorted. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): top-down vs three-quarter angle, how props frame without crowding, where soft daylight casts believable contact shadows. STYLING + LIGHT CONSTRAINTS: soft directional window daylight, accurate contact and cast shadows, balanced negative space, cohesive palette, realistic material textures on props; BAN floating items with no shadow, cluttered composition, gibberish or altered label text, repeated identical props. OUTPUT FORMAT (print exactly these three labeled blocks, nothing before or after): 1) NANO-BANANA / DALL-E PROMPT: one conversational paragraph describing the angle, soft daylight, prop styling, and the literal line 'preserve the exact product, logo and label unchanged'. 2) MIDJOURNEY V7: one line ordered Subject > Props > Lighting > Style, ending exactly with --ar 1:1 --style raw --s 100 --hd. 3) FLUX KONTEXT EDIT: one 'add styled props and a soft daylight shadow around the product while keeping the product itself unchanged' instruction. Output nothing else.
h: Upload the product, then name the [PRODUCT], a [SCENE/THEME] and 2-3 [PROPS].

p: ROLE: You are a photographer who deliberately shoots imperfect point-and-shoot film so images read as found memories. TASK: Using the uploaded photo as the single source of identity, restyle this exact person as a 2000s DISPOSABLE-CAMERA flash snapshot with identity preserved. Scene/vibe: [SCENE/VIBE]. Location: [LOCATION]. Corner timestamp to render: [TIMESTAMP]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): the precise tell-stack of a real direct-flash photo - hot flash falloff, slight motion, heavy grain, color shift, vignette - and how each one defeats the too-perfect AI look. FILM CONSTRAINTS: harsh direct on-camera flash with bright skin falloff, visible grain, mild light leaks, slight overexposure near the flash, soft focus at the edges, white Polaroid-style border, a small orange corner timestamp; keep real skin and the exact same face; BAN crisp studio sharpness, clean creamy bokeh, over-clean skin, identity drift. OUTPUT FORMAT (print exactly these three labeled blocks, nothing before or after): 1) NANO-BANANA / DALL-E PROMPT: one conversational paragraph describing the disposable-camera flash look, grain, light leaks, white border, the literal corner timestamp '[TIMESTAMP]', and the line 'keep the exact same face'. 2) MIDJOURNEY V7: one line ordered Subject > Scene > Flash Look > Film, ending exactly with --ar 4:5 --style raw --s 200 --hd. 3) FLUX KONTEXT EDIT: one 'add harsh on-camera flash, film grain, light leaks and a white Polaroid border with the timestamp "[TIMESTAMP]" while keeping the same face and pose' instruction. Output nothing else.
h: Upload the photo, then set the [SCENE/VIBE], an optional [LOCATION] and a corner [TIMESTAMP].

p: ROLE: You are a portrait art director and prompt engineer. CONTEXT: My rough goal is [WHAT I WANT]. Do NOT write the image prompt yet. STEP 1 - INTERVIEW: ask me 5-7 diagnostic questions, ONE at a time, to nail the spec I have not thought through - purpose and platform plus aspect ratio, mood and wardrobe, lighting style (studio vs natural vs cinematic), lens and crop, background, and whether identity must be preserved from an uploaded photo. For each question, also propose the answer YOU would recommend so I can simply reply 'yes, go with that'. Wait for my reply before asking the next. STEP 2 - after I answer all (or say 'use your picks'), THINK silently about how the choices combine, then deliver. OUTPUT FORMAT (only at Step 2, print exactly these four labeled blocks): 1) SPEC RECAP: 4-6 bullet lines of the locked decisions. 2) NANO-BANANA / DALL-E PROMPT: one conversational paragraph with a named lens, lighting, and 'keep the same face' if a photo was uploaded. 3) MIDJOURNEY V7: one line ordered Subject > Environment > Lighting > Lens, ending with --ar, --style raw and --hd. 4) FLUX KONTEXT EDIT: one 'change only X, preserve the rest' instruction. Begin now with question 1 only, and nothing else.
h: Upload your photo (optional) and state your rough goal in [WHAT I WANT], then just answer the questions it asks.

p: ROLE: You are a product photographer shooting premium Japanese collectible figures. TASK: Using the uploaded photo as the single source of identity, create a hyper-real 1/7-SCALE COLLECTIBLE FIGURINE of this exact person, keeping the face, hairstyle and outfit clearly recognizable. Pose/theme: [THEME/POSE]. Box brand vibe: [BOX BRAND VIBE]. SCENE: the figure stands on a round transparent acrylic base on a desk; behind it a PC monitor shows the 3D modeling viewport of the same figure; beside it a premium printed figure box reads '[YOUR NAME]'. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): which details read at figure scale, how studio product light hits sculpted PVC, how to keep the box text crisp and legible. MATERIAL + LIGHT CONSTRAINTS: realistic painted-PVC plastic sheen, sharp sculpt detail, soft studio product lighting, a true contact shadow on the desk, shallow depth on the box; BAN warped or gibberish box text, a second figure, a floating base, human-skin realism on the figure (it is plastic). OUTPUT FORMAT (print exactly these three labeled blocks, nothing before or after): 1) NANO-BANANA / DALL-E PROMPT: one conversational paragraph for the full scene with the literal lines 'render the box title "[YOUR NAME]" clearly' and 'keep the same face on the figure'. 2) MIDJOURNEY V7: one line ordered Subject > Pose > Scene > Lighting, ending exactly with --ar 4:5 --style raw --s 120 --hd. 3) FLUX KONTEXT EDIT: one 'keep the figure and box title "[YOUR NAME]" sharp and legible while preserving the same face' instruction. Output nothing else.
h: Upload a clear full or half-body photo, then set [YOUR NAME], a [THEME/POSE] and an optional [BOX BRAND VIBE].

p: ROLE: You are a prompt-systems engineer building a reusable photoreal recipe. TASK: Take my inputs - subject: [SUBJECT]; lighting: [LIGHTING]; camera: [CAMERA]; on-image text in quotes: [TEXT] - and produce a portable image recipe. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): keep the schema flat and literal about identity and structure, never flowery; every field must be a concrete phrase. OUTPUT FORMAT (print exactly these four labeled blocks, in this order, nothing else): 1) JSON RECIPE: a single flat JSON object with keys subject, wardrobe, setting, lighting, camera (lens plus aperture), mood, color_palette, aspect_ratio, render_text - each value a concrete phrase; if no on-image text was given, set render_text to "". 2) NANO-BANANA / DALL-E: the recipe rewritten as one conversational paragraph (Nano Banana reasons over plain English; put any on-image words in DOUBLE QUOTES; add 'keep the same face' if a photo is uploaded). 3) MIDJOURNEY V7: the same recipe as one line ordered Subject > Setting > Lighting > Lens, ending with --ar (use the recipe's aspect_ratio value) --style raw --hd. 4) FLUX KONTEXT EDIT: one 'change only the swappable field, preserve the rest' instruction naming whichever recipe field is most likely to be re-rolled. HARD RULE: the subject and identity wording must be byte-for-byte identical across all four blocks. Output nothing else.
h: Fill the schema fields: [SUBJECT], [LIGHTING], [CAMERA], and an optional on-image [TEXT].

p: ROLE: You are a lifestyle commercial photographer who shoots products being used by real people. TASK: Using the uploaded photo as the exact product reference, build a hyper-real LIFESTYLE 'PRODUCT IN USE' shot of this product: [PRODUCT]. Context: [USE CONTEXT]. Hands/model: [MODEL/HANDS]. NON-NEGOTIABLE: keep the product's real shape, material, color and label text legible and unchanged. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): how a human interacts with it naturally, where soft daylight falls, what shallow depth keeps focus on the product. REALISM CONSTRAINTS: natural hand anatomy with the correct finger count, soft directional window light, candid framing, a believable environment with gentle background blur, accurate contact shadows where the product meets surfaces; BAN extra or fused fingers, a warped or relabeled product, a stiff staged feel, gibberish text. OUTPUT FORMAT (print exactly these three labeled blocks, nothing before or after): 1) NANO-BANANA / DALL-E PROMPT: one conversational paragraph naming a 50mm or 35mm lens at f/2.8, soft daylight, the use moment, and the literal line 'preserve the exact product, logo and label, with natural correct hands'. 2) MIDJOURNEY V7: one line ordered Subject > Context > Lighting > Lens, ending exactly with --ar 4:5 --style raw --s 100 --hd. 3) FLUX KONTEXT EDIT: one 'place the product naturally in a hand within [USE CONTEXT] while keeping the product and label unchanged' instruction. Output nothing else.
h: Upload the product, then name the [PRODUCT], the [USE CONTEXT] and an optional [MODEL/HANDS] detail.

p: ROLE: You are a portrait photographer who recreates authentic period photography. TASK: Using the uploaded photo as the single source of identity, restyle this exact person into a [DECADE] portrait, keeping their face, age and identity fully recognizable. Camera/film: [FILM STOCK/CAMERA]. Setting: [SETTING]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): what makes this era read true - grain structure, color shift, wardrobe, hairstyle and the lighting tech of the time - without altering the person. PERIOD + REALISM CONSTRAINTS: era-accurate film grain and color cast, period-correct wardrobe and hair, soft on-camera or studio lighting matching the decade, a slight imperfection so it reads as a real photo not clean AI; keep real skin texture; BAN modern over-sharpening, any change to face shape or age, anachronistic clothing, identity drift. OUTPUT FORMAT (print exactly these three labeled blocks, nothing before or after): 1) NANO-BANANA / DALL-E PROMPT: one conversational paragraph naming the decade, the film look, an 85mm or 50mm lens, period grain, and the literal line 'keep the exact same face and identity'. 2) MIDJOURNEY V7: one line ordered Subject > Era Wardrobe > Lighting > Film, ending exactly with --ar 4:5 --style raw --s 180 --hd. 3) FLUX KONTEXT EDIT: one 'apply an authentic [DECADE] film look and wardrobe while preserving the exact face, age and pose' instruction. Output nothing else.
h: Upload the photo, then pick the [DECADE], an optional [FILM STOCK/CAMERA] and [SETTING].

p: ROLE: You are a commercial product photographer and retoucher shooting a flagship hero image for a paid ad. TASK: Take the attached [PRODUCT] and render it as a single scroll-stopping hero shot on a clean seamless studio background. THINK FIRST (do this silently, do not print it): decide the camera angle that flatters this product's shape, the main light direction, and where empty space should sit so a headline can be added later. THEN GENERATE: place the product centered-but-off-balance with deliberate negative space on one side, set against a smooth seamless backdrop tinted toward [BRAND COLOR]; light it with one large soft key from the upper-left, a subtle fill, and a crisp edge highlight that separates it from the background; add a soft natural contact shadow and a faint mirror reflection under the product; keep textures, labels and materials photoreal and tack-sharp with a shallow but product-covering depth of field. CONSTRAINTS: keep the product's real shape, color, proportions and any visible branding accurate — do not invent a different product; one product only, no extra props, no added text or logos, no warped edges or melted labels, realistic studio lighting (no flat AI glow). OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished hero image only in [ASPECT RATIO], then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw --v 6'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the backdrop color or camera angle.
h: Attach a product photo (or describe it), name the [PRODUCT], then optionally set a [BRAND COLOR] for the backdrop and the platform [ASPECT RATIO].

p: ROLE: You are a lifestyle commercial photographer who shoots products being used by real people. TASK: Show the attached [PRODUCT] naturally in use within [SETTING], as if captured mid-moment for a brand campaign. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide who is plausibly using it, the time of day, and the one believable gesture that shows the product working. THEN GENERATE: place the product as the clear focal point, interacting with a real human hand or being used at the scene, surrounded by a few authentic everyday details of [SETTING] that are softly out of focus; light it with warm directional window light, gentle shadows and a relaxed, candid feel carrying the [MOOD]; keep the product sharp and correctly colored while the background falls into a creamy shallow-depth blur. CONSTRAINTS: keep the product's real design, label and proportions accurate; show realistic skin and a natural five-finger hand if a hand appears (no extra fingers), authentic lighting, no staged stock-photo stiffness, no added text or logos, one main product only. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished lifestyle image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the setting or the time of day.
h: Name the [PRODUCT], set the [SETTING] (e.g. sunlit kitchen counter, morning desk), and optionally the [MOOD] (e.g. calm, energetic).

p: ROLE: You are a direct-response ad designer building an honest, eye-catching before/after visual. TASK: Create one image split into two matched panels showing [SUBJECT] in the [BEFORE STATE] on the left and the improved [AFTER STATE] on the right, driven by the [PRODUCT]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the single visible difference that proves the result, and keep camera angle, crop, distance and lighting identical across both sides so the comparison is fair. THEN GENERATE: render two panels at equal size separated by a thin clean divider; the left panel shows the realistic, unflattering-but-not-exaggerated before; the right panel shows the visibly better after with the same subject, framing and light; add small, legible 'Before' and 'After' labels in clean sans-serif at the top corners; keep lighting soft and consistent and the product subtly present or implied. CONSTRAINTS: change only the result, not the person, angle, or scene; keep any real product design accurate; realistic and believable (no impossible or medically misleading claims), no fake percentages or stats, correctly spelled labels only, no other text. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished before/after image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 1:1 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to make the contrast stronger or change the subject.
h: Name the [PRODUCT], describe the [BEFORE STATE] and the [AFTER STATE], and optionally the [SUBJECT] (e.g. skin, a messy desk, dull hair).

p: ROLE: You are an art director producing a seasonal hero image for a holiday ad campaign. TASK: Stage the attached [PRODUCT] in a festive [HOLIDAY/SEASON] scene that feels warm, premium and on-brand. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): pick 2-3 tasteful seasonal cues (no clutter), the dominant [PALETTE], and where to leave clean copy space for an offer line. THEN GENERATE: hero the product front and center, surrounded by a few elegant seasonal props appropriate to the occasion, with a soft out-of-focus background of warm bokeh lights or season-appropriate texture; light it cinematically with a warm key and gentle rim light, add subtle festive atmosphere (soft glow, faint sparkle or seasonal foliage), and keep one calm area of negative space for headline and offer text to be added later. CONSTRAINTS: keep the product design, label and colors accurate; tasteful and uncluttered (no kitsch overload), realistic materials and lighting, no added text or logos in the image itself, one main product only, family-friendly. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished holiday hero image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to switch the holiday or simplify the props.
h: Name the [PRODUCT], pick the [HOLIDAY/SEASON] (e.g. Christmas, Diwali, Valentine's), and optionally a [PALETTE].

p: ROLE: You are a high-speed commercial photographer specializing in frozen-motion product shots. TASK: Capture the attached [PRODUCT] in a dramatic mid-action moment with [INGREDIENTS/ELEMENTS] suspended around it as if frozen by a flash. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the direction of motion, which elements orbit the product, and the light that makes droplets and surfaces pop. THEN GENERATE: place the product as the sharp hero, surrounded by a crisp frozen splash, flying [INGREDIENTS/ELEMENTS] and suspended droplets caught in crystal-clear high-speed detail; set it against a clean studio [BACKDROP COLOR] with a subtle gradient; light it with hard directional flash plus rim light so every droplet and edge sparkles, and keep a strong sense of energy and motion while the product stays perfectly readable. CONSTRAINTS: keep the product's real shape, label and colors accurate; physically believable splash and motion (no smeared or melted shapes), photoreal liquid and surfaces, no added text or logos, one main product only. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished frozen-motion image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the flying elements or the backdrop color.
h: Name the [PRODUCT], list the [INGREDIENTS/ELEMENTS] to fly around it (e.g. mint, orange slices, water droplets), and optionally a [BACKDROP COLOR].

p: ROLE: You are a still-life stylist shooting a clean overhead flat-lay (knolling) for social ads. TASK: Arrange the attached [PRODUCT] with its [SUPPORTING ITEMS] in a neat top-down composition photographed straight from above. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the grid logic, which item is the hero, and how to balance color and spacing so the eye lands on the product first. THEN GENERATE: shoot perfectly top-down with the product as the clear centerpiece, surrounded by its supporting items arranged at tidy right angles with even, deliberate spacing on a flat [SURFACE COLOR] surface; light it softly and evenly so shadows are gentle and consistent; keep every object crisp, well-separated and color-accurate, with balanced negative space and a satisfying, organized rhythm. CONSTRAINTS: keep each item's real shape, label and proportions accurate; clean even lighting (no harsh hotspots), realistic materials, no overlapping clutter, no added text or logos, true overhead angle (no perspective tilt). OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished flat-lay image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 1:1 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the surface color or swap a supporting item.
h: Name the [PRODUCT], list the [SUPPORTING ITEMS] to arrange around it, and optionally a [SURFACE COLOR].

p: ROLE: You are a macro product photographer who sells quality through extreme detail. TASK: Shoot an ultra-close macro frame of the attached [PRODUCT] that hero's its [DETAIL]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the exact spot on the product that proves craftsmanship, the angle that reveals texture, and the light that rakes across the surface. THEN GENERATE: fill the frame with a tight macro view of the chosen [DETAIL], showing fine surface texture, material grain and micro-highlights in razor-sharp focus, with a beautifully soft fall-off blur just behind the focal plane; light it with [MOOD LIGHT] raking from the side to carve out depth and texture; keep colors true and the surface tactile enough to almost feel. CONSTRAINTS: keep the material, color and texture authentic to the real product; photoreal micro-detail (no plastic-smooth AI sheen), believable lighting, no added text or logos, one focal detail only. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished macro image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to move the focus to a different detail or change the light.
h: Name the [PRODUCT], pick the [DETAIL] to feature (e.g. stitching, droplets, brushed metal), and optionally the [MOOD LIGHT].

p: ROLE: You are a high-end product photographer lighting a flagship launch image. TASK: Render the attached [PRODUCT] floating in space inside a dark, dramatic studio with sculpted lighting. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the float angle that looks most premium, the single key beam's direction, and how the accent light should kiss one edge. THEN GENERATE: suspend the product weightlessly against a moody [BACKDROP TONE] gradient with subtle vignetting; carve it out with one dramatic key light and a [LIGHT COLOR] rim or accent that traces an edge; add a soft floor shadow or faint reflection beneath to ground the float; keep the product razor-sharp, color-accurate and clearly the only thing lit, with rich blacks and controlled, intentional highlights. CONSTRAINTS: keep the product's real shape, label and finish accurate; cinematic but believable lighting (no glowing halo of nowhere), photoreal materials, no added text or logos, one product only, no busy background. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished floating hero image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the backdrop tone or accent color.
h: Name the [PRODUCT], set the [BACKDROP TONE] (e.g. deep charcoal, midnight blue), and optionally a [LIGHT COLOR] for the accent beam.

p: ROLE: You are an ad art director laying out a finished static ad creative. TASK: Compose a single ad-ready image of the attached [PRODUCT] with a clean packshot on one side and clearly reserved space for a headline and an offer badge on the other, in a [BRAND VIBE] style. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the layout grid, which side holds the product, and how to keep the copy zone calm and uncluttered. THEN GENERATE: place a crisp, well-lit packshot of the product occupying roughly one half or third of the frame, set on a clean on-brand background that matches the [BRAND VIBE]; leave the remaining area as smooth negative space with a subtle visual cue (a soft block, gentle gradient or simple shape) marking where a headline goes and a small circular zone where an offer badge would sit; balance the composition so the eye flows from product to copy space. Do not write the actual marketing copy — leave those zones empty and clean. THEN, beneath the image, suggest one short headline and one offer-badge line as plain text I could add. CONSTRAINTS: keep the product accurate; leave the headline/badge areas free of generated gibberish text, no fake logos, realistic product lighting, one product only. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished layout image (with empty copy zones) only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then two short text lines (a headline and an offer) I can place into the empty zones.
h: Name the [PRODUCT], set the [BRAND VIBE] (e.g. minimal premium, bold playful), and optionally the platform [ASPECT RATIO].

p: ROLE: You are an outdoor lifestyle and adventure brand photographer. TASK: Place the attached [PRODUCT] into a vivid [ENVIRONMENT] hero shot that feels aspirational and alive. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the product's natural placement in the scene, the weather and atmosphere, and the light that makes it feel like a real campaign frame. THEN GENERATE: hero the product convincingly within [ENVIRONMENT] at [TIME OF DAY], integrated naturally with the terrain or surface (resting on rock, sand, pavement or held in a scene); surround it with authentic atmosphere — golden light, mist, spray, dust or motion blur in the background — while keeping the product sharp and color-accurate; light it with believable natural directional light and add a cinematic, slightly graded color mood. CONSTRAINTS: keep the product's real design and proportions accurate; realistic environment and scale (no floating or wrong-size product), authentic lighting and weather, no added text or logos, one main product, family-friendly. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished outdoor hero image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 16:9 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the environment or time of day.
h: Name the [PRODUCT], set the [ENVIRONMENT] (e.g. mountain trail, surf beach), and optionally the [TIME OF DAY].

p: ROLE: You are recreating an authentic user-generated content (UGC) phone photo for a native-feeling ad. TASK: Make the attached [PRODUCT] look like a genuine customer snapshot taken on a phone in an [EVERYDAY SETTING]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the casual angle, the believable everyday clutter nearby, and the natural indoor light that sells authenticity. THEN GENERATE: show the product casually placed or held in a real, slightly imperfect [EVERYDAY SETTING] with normal background details (a coffee mug, charging cable, a window); use the look of a modern phone camera at [TIME OF DAY] — natural soft indoor lighting, a slightly candid composition, gentle realistic depth, and the unpolished authenticity of a real post (not studio-perfect); keep the product clearly visible and recognizable. CONSTRAINTS: keep the product accurate; deliberately authentic, not glossy or staged (no studio sweep, no perfect symmetry), realistic phone-photo lighting, natural five-finger hand if one appears, no added text or logos, one main product. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished UGC-style image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the setting or make it look even more casual.
h: Name the [PRODUCT], set the [EVERYDAY SETTING] (e.g. messy desk, bathroom shelf, car cupholder), and optionally the [TIME OF DAY].

p: ROLE: You are a bold commercial art director designing a single-color statement ad. TASK: Render the attached [PRODUCT] in a striking monochrome composition built around one dominant [BRAND COLOR]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide how to keep the product the focal hero while everything else leans into the single hue, and where one optional accent prop adds rhythm. THEN GENERATE: set the product against a saturated, matching [BRAND COLOR] backdrop and surface so the whole frame reads as one bold color world; keep the product crisp and clearly the hero, using subtle tonal shifts, shadow and highlight within the same hue to create depth; optionally add a single [ACCENT PROP] in the same palette for visual interest; light it cleanly so the product's form and label stay readable against the color wash. CONSTRAINTS: keep the product's real label colors readable even within the monochrome scheme; clean confident composition (not muddy), realistic lighting and materials, no added text or logos, one product plus at most one prop. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished color-pop image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 1:1 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the color or remove the accent prop.
h: Name the [PRODUCT], pick the dominant [BRAND COLOR], and optionally a single [ACCENT PROP].

p: ROLE: You are an e-commerce product photographer shooting a clean, trustworthy conversion image. TASK: Show the attached [PRODUCT] held naturally in a single human hand against a soft, clean [BACKGROUND TONE] background. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the grip that best shows the product's front and scale, the hand angle, and the light that keeps both hand and product flattering. THEN GENERATE: present the product gently held in one realistic [SKIN/HAND STYLE] hand, fingers relaxed and natural, positioned so the product's key face and details are clearly visible and its true size reads instantly; set it against a smooth, minimal [BACKGROUND TONE] backdrop with soft even light, a gentle shadow under the hand, and shallow depth keeping the product sharp; keep skin natural and the product color-accurate. CONSTRAINTS: keep the product's real design, label and proportions accurate; render a believable hand with exactly five natural fingers (no extra or fused fingers), realistic soft lighting, no added text or logos, one product and one hand only. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished hand-held product image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 1:1 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the background tone or the hand angle.
h: Name the [PRODUCT], set the [BACKGROUND TONE] (e.g. soft beige, light grey), and optionally a [SKIN/HAND STYLE].

p: ROLE: You are a vaporwave digital artist building an iconic 1984-meets-internet dreamscape. TASK: Place [SUBJECT] inside a full vaporwave scene using a [PALETTE] color story, anchored by a [MOOD ELEMENT]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide where the horizon line sits, how the neon grid converges to a vanishing point, and which one or two retro motifs sell the era without clutter. THEN GENERATE: build a glowing neon wireframe grid floor that recedes to a low horizon under a striped gradient sunset; add a banded wireframe or chrome sun, distant palm or pyramid silhouettes, and the [MOOD ELEMENT] floating tastefully; render the whole frame in saturated pink, cyan and purple with soft VHS bloom, faint scanlines and a hazy chromatic-aberration edge for that CRT-photographed feel; keep the subject clearly the hero, lit by the neon ambience. CONSTRAINTS: era-accurate 80s palette only (no muddy modern gradients), tasteful not chaotic, keep the subject recognizable, no readable logos, optional faint Japanese-style katakana decoration only if it stays decorative. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished vaporwave image only in a 4:5 frame, then on a new line a Midjourney param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw --v 6'), then one sentence I can paste back to swap the palette or the mood element.
h: Name the [SUBJECT] to feature, optionally set a [PALETTE] (e.g. hot-pink + cyan, teal + magenta) and a [MOOD ELEMENT] (e.g. floating Roman bust, retro car).

p: ROLE: You are a film photographer shooting on a vintage 35mm SLR with expired 1970s color stock. TASK: Photograph [SUBJECT/SCENE] in a [SETTING] under [LIGHT] so it reads as a genuine faded 70s snapshot. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the casual amateur framing, the warm color cast the aged film would give, and where a soft light leak or vignette would naturally fall. THEN GENERATE: render the scene with warm amber-and-mustard tones, slightly lifted blacks, gentle desaturation and a faded highlight roll-off; add fine organic film grain, soft focus falloff, a subtle corner light leak and faint halation around bright spots; compose it like a real handheld family photo of the era — slightly off-center, honest, unposed. CONSTRAINTS: keep it photoreal (not illustrated), era-true 70s warmth (no teal-orange modern grade), believable period clothing, hair and objects, no text or watermarks, no perfect studio lighting. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished film-look photo only in a 3:2 frame, then on a new line a Midjourney param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 3:2 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the setting or push the fade stronger or weaker.
h: Describe the [SUBJECT/SCENE] to photograph, optionally set a [SETTING] (e.g. sunlit kitchen, roadside motel) and the [LIGHT] (e.g. warm afternoon, overcast).

p: ROLE: You are a risograph printmaker designing a limited-run 2-color print. TASK: Illustrate [SUBJECT] as an authentic risograph poster using only [TWO INKS] on [PAPER]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide how the two ink layers overlap to create a third blended tone, which shapes carry each color, and where intentional misregistration adds charm. THEN GENERATE: build a bold, simplified illustration in just those two spot colors, layering flat shapes and visible coarse halftone dot patterns; add a slight 1-2px misregistration between the layers, subtle ink texture and grain, and the warm matte tooth of the chosen recycled paper showing through; keep the composition graphic and poster-like with generous flat areas. CONSTRAINTS: strictly two ink colors plus the paper (no full-color rendering, no smooth gradients except via halftone), keep it flat and screenprint-honest, leave clean space for an optional headline, no photoreal shading, no extra logos. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished risograph print only in a 4:5 frame, then on a new line a Midjourney param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to swap the two inks or the paper stock.
h: Describe the [SUBJECT] to illustrate, optionally pick the [TWO INKS] (e.g. fluoro pink + teal, orange + navy) and a [PAPER] (e.g. cream, kraft).

p: ROLE: You are a mid-century commercial illustrator designing a golden-age travel poster. TASK: Create a 1950s-style travel poster for [DESTINATION] in a [PALETTE], built around a [HERO MOTIF]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the single iconic view that says this place at a glance, how to simplify it into bold flat shapes, and where to reserve a clean band for a future title. THEN GENERATE: compose a confident flat-graphic scene with strong geometric simplification, layered silhouettes and subtle screen-print texture; use a tight harmonious mid-century palette with warm and cool accents; add gentle paper grain and the faint imperfections of a vintage lithograph; render the [HERO MOTIF] as the focal element with stylized light and crisp edges. CONSTRAINTS: flat poster illustration (not photoreal), era-true limited palette and shapes, leave clean negative space for a headline, no modern UI or gradients, no readable logos unless decorative, keep it tasteful and timeless. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished travel poster only in a 2:3 frame, then on a new line a Midjourney param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 2:3 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the destination motif or the palette.
h: Name the [DESTINATION] to feature, optionally set a [PALETTE] (e.g. teal + coral + cream) and a [HERO MOTIF] (e.g. vintage airplane, ocean liner, mountain).

p: ROLE: You are a 1950s pulp paperback cover painter working in dramatic gouache and airbrush. TASK: Paint [SCENE] as a vintage pulp sci-fi cover with a [GENRE TWIST] in a [PALETTE]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the single high-drama moment, the diagonal that pulls the eye, and where the over-the-top lighting comes from. THEN GENERATE: paint a bold, theatrical scene with a heroic figure mid-action, retro-futurist ships, rayguns or alien vistas; use confident painterly brushwork, airbrushed glows, deep cast shadows and that slightly garish over-saturated pulp palette; add subtle aged-paper texture, faint print dot pattern and a touch of cover wear at the edges. CONSTRAINTS: hand-painted pulp look (not photoreal, not 3D), era-true 50s costuming and tech, leave a clear band at top or bottom for a title and a faint price corner, keep it bold and dramatic, no real-world brand names, family-safe. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished pulp cover only in a 2:3 frame, then on a new line a Midjourney param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 2:3 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the genre twist or palette.
h: Describe the [SCENE] or hero, optionally set a [GENRE TWIST] (e.g. Martian invasion, atomic ray-gun) and a [PALETTE] (e.g. acid green + crimson).

p: ROLE: You are recreating a paused frame from a 1980s consumer camcorder VHS tape. TASK: Render [SUBJECT/MOMENT] in a [SCENE] as an authentic VHS camcorder still with a [TIMESTAMP] overlay. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the casual home-video framing, how the analog signal degrades the image, and where the on-screen text would sit. THEN GENERATE: render a slightly soft, low-resolution image with horizontal interlacing scanlines, faint tracking distortion bands, chroma smear and color bleed (especially in reds), mild noise and a warm slightly washed exposure; overlay a glowing chunky camcorder UI timestamp and 'PLAY' or 'REC' style text in the corner using a period dot-font; frame it like a handheld home video, imperfect and candid. CONSTRAINTS: keep the analog degradation believable and not overdone, era-true 80s clothing and objects, the overlay text small and corner-placed, no modern UI, no watermark, family-safe. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished VHS still only in a 4:3 frame, then on a new line a Midjourney param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:3 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the scene or the timestamp.
h: Describe the [SUBJECT/MOMENT] being filmed, optionally set the [SCENE] (e.g. backyard birthday, neon arcade) and a [TIMESTAMP] (e.g. JUL 14 1987).

p: ROLE: You are a cel animator producing a frame in the style of a 1990s hand-painted anime. TASK: Render [SUBJECT/CHARACTER] in a [SCENE] with a [MOOD], as an authentic retro anime cel still. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the cinematic frame composition, the limited cel-shaded color blocking, and how the painted background sets the atmosphere. THEN GENERATE: draw the character with clean ink linework and flat 90s-style cel shading (two or three tone steps, hard shadow edges) over a softly painted gouache-look background; use the muted, slightly desaturated retro anime palette of the era; add subtle film grain, faint analog color bleed and a soft glow as if scanned from old film. CONSTRAINTS: hand-drawn cel look (not glossy modern digital, not 3D), era-true 90s anime proportions and shading, atmospheric not busy, no on-screen text or subtitles, family-safe. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished anime cel still only in a 4:3 frame, then on a new line a Midjourney param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:3 --style raw --niji 6'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the scene or the mood.
h: Describe the [SUBJECT/CHARACTER], optionally set a [SCENE] (e.g. rainy neon street, sunset rooftop) and a [MOOD] (e.g. melancholy, action).

p: ROLE: You are a mid-century advertising art director creating a magazine print ad. TASK: Design a vintage [ERA] print advertisement for [PRODUCT] in a [PALETTE]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the single aspirational message, the cheerful staging of product and people, and where to reserve a clean band for a headline and tagline. THEN GENERATE: compose a warm, optimistic ad with the product as hero, a smiling period-styled model interacting with it, painted-illustration or early-color-photo finish, and confident flat color blocks; use the era-true palette, subtle halftone print texture, slight paper aging and a reserved title area; light it bright and inviting like an aspirational lifestyle promise. CONSTRAINTS: era-accurate clothing, hair, props and styling for the chosen decade, leave clean space for headline + tagline, keep the product recognizable but no real modern brand marks, no jarring modern elements, tasteful and family-safe. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished print ad only in a 4:5 frame, then on a new line a Midjourney param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the era or the palette.
h: Name the [PRODUCT] to advertise, optionally set the [ERA] (e.g. 1955, 1968) and a [PALETTE] (e.g. turquoise + cherry red + cream).

p: ROLE: You are a synthwave cover artist designing an outrun album sleeve. TASK: Create a retro synthwave album cover about [THEME/SUBJECT] in a [PALETTE], centered on a [HERO ELEMENT]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the horizon placement, how the neon grid streaks toward the vanishing point, and where a chrome title would sit. THEN GENERATE: render a striped neon sunset with a banded sun, a perspective grid of glowing lines racing to the horizon, distant mountain or city silhouettes and the [HERO ELEMENT] backlit in rim light; coat it in saturated 80s neon, subtle starfield, lens flare, grain and faint chromatic aberration; reserve a clean central or top zone where a chrome metallic title could go. CONSTRAINTS: era-true outrun palette and motifs, bold and cinematic not cluttered, leave room for a title, no readable real logos, keep it square and balanced. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished album cover only in a 1:1 frame, then on a new line a Midjourney param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 1:1 --style raw --v 6'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the hero element or the palette.
h: Describe the [THEME/SUBJECT] of the cover, optionally pick a [PALETTE] (e.g. magenta + orange, blue + pink) and a [HERO ELEMENT] (e.g. sports car, lone figure).

p: ROLE: You are a 1920s Art Deco poster designer in the golden age of graphic elegance. TASK: Design an Art Deco poster featuring [SUBJECT] in a [PALETTE], built on a [MOTIF]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the strong symmetry or vertical thrust, the geometric framing devices, and where a title plate of clean negative space belongs. THEN GENERATE: compose a stately, symmetrical layout with bold geometric forms, streamlined stylization, sunburst or stepped chevron ornament and elegant thin gold linework; use a rich limited palette with metallic gold accents, flat luxe color and subtle screen-print or foil texture; render the [SUBJECT] in confident stylized geometry as the centerpiece. CONSTRAINTS: true Deco geometry and restraint (not busy, not modern flat-design), elegant limited palette, leave a clean panel for a title, period-accurate styling, no readable modern logos, tasteful. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished Art Deco poster only in a 2:3 frame, then on a new line a Midjourney param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 2:3 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the motif or the palette.
h: Describe the [SUBJECT] to feature, optionally set a [PALETTE] (e.g. gold + black + emerald) and a [MOTIF] (e.g. sunburst, skyscraper, chevrons).

p: ROLE: You are an early photographer producing a [ERA] daguerreotype or tintype portrait. TASK: Render [SUBJECT] as an authentic antique photograph in a [TONE] finish. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the formal, still composition the long exposure demands, the period dress and the gentle vignette an old plate would have. THEN GENERATE: render a soft monochrome (silver or sepia) image with the slightly stiff, solemn stillness of a long-exposure portrait; add a vignetted oval framing, shallow soft focus, fine plate scratches, tarnish blooms and uneven aging at the edges; keep lighting soft, directional and window-lit, with period-accurate clothing, hair and setting. CONSTRAINTS: monochrome only (silver/sepia), era-true 1800s styling and props (no modern items), believable plate damage and vignette, solemn and respectful, no text, no anachronisms, family-safe. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished antique portrait only in a 4:5 frame, then on a new line a Midjourney param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the era or the tone.
h: Describe the [SUBJECT] to portray, optionally set the [ERA] (e.g. 1850s, 1870s) and a [TONE] (e.g. silver, sepia tintype).

p: ROLE: You are a 1960s-70s psychedelic poster artist drawing for a legendary concert hall. TASK: Create a psychedelic poster about [SUBJECT/THEME] in a [PALETTE], built around a [CENTRAL MOTIF]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the swirling symmetry, how the warping liquid forms fill every inch, and where vibrating complementary colors create that optical buzz. THEN GENERATE: draw dense, hand-rendered flowing line art with melting swirls, kaleidoscopic mandala symmetry and a hypnotic [CENTRAL MOTIF]; use intense vibrating complementary colors that shimmer at their edges, flat saturated fills and ornate borders; reserve organic liquid-lettering zones (drawn as warped shapes, no real legible words needed) and add subtle aged-paper texture. CONSTRAINTS: hand-drawn psychedelic look (not 3D, not photoreal), dense ornate fill edge to edge, era-true 60s-70s palette and forms, leave warped space where a title could melt in, no real logos, tasteful. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished psychedelic poster only in a 2:3 frame, then on a new line a Midjourney param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 2:3 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the central motif or the palette.
h: Describe the [SUBJECT/THEME] of the poster, optionally pick a [PALETTE] (e.g. orange + magenta + lime) and a [CENTRAL MOTIF] (e.g. sun face, peacock, eye).

p: ROLE: You are a retro game pixel artist building a frame for a [BIT ERA] cartridge title. TASK: Create a pixel-art game scene of [SCENE/SUBJECT] in a [SETTING], true to the [BIT ERA] hardware. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the tile-based composition, the tightly limited palette that era's console allowed, and how dithering stands in for gradients. THEN GENERATE: render crisp, chunky pixels on a clean grid with a small era-accurate palette, dithered shading, clear readable sprites and a parallax-layered background; add a simple faux HUD bar (score, hearts, generic icons) drawn in matching pixel style; keep the look sharp and aliased like real low-res sprite art, with strong silhouettes and limited colors per sprite. CONSTRAINTS: authentic low-resolution pixel grid (no anti-aliased smoothness, no high-res detail sneaking in), palette and sprite size true to the chosen era, readable and game-like, no real game logos or copyrighted characters, family-safe. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished pixel-art scene only in a 4:3 frame, then on a new line a Midjourney param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:3 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the setting or the bit era.
h: Describe the [SCENE/SUBJECT] to build, optionally set the [BIT ERA] (e.g. 8-bit NES, 16-bit SNES) and a [SETTING] (e.g. forest level, neon city).

p: ROLE: You are a period-accurate portrait stylist and retoucher working inside a conversational image editor (GPT-4o / Gemini). TASK: Take the attached selfie [SELFIE] and restyle it as if the same person were photographed in the [ERA] — keep their identity, but change everything around it to fit the decade. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): list what actually signals that era — the hairstyle, the eyewear, the fabric and cut of clothing, the color grading and grain of the era's cameras, the background props — then apply only those. THEN EDIT: restyle the hair and wardrobe to a believable [ERA] look, place them in a [SETTING] that fits the period, and grade the whole frame to match that era's photography (film stock, color cast, grain, lens character). Carry a [MOOD] through the lighting and expression. CONSTRAINTS: keep the same face geometry, skin tone, eye color and core features unmistakably recognizable — do NOT beautify, slim, or morph them into a different person; one person only; period-accurate details (no anachronistic logos, phones, or modern clothing); natural five-finger hands; no text or watermarks. OUTPUT FORMAT: return the finished era-restyled portrait only, then on a new line give me one short follow-up sentence I can paste back to push the era earlier/later or swap the outfit.
h: Attach your selfie, name the [ERA] (e.g. 1970s, 1920s, Y2K 2000s), and optionally a [SETTING] and [MOOD].

TASK: produce a single photoreal team-celebration frame of [SPORT/TEAM] in [CELEBRATION] at [SETTING]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the emotional focal point of the pile-up and the burst of motion that radiates joy outward through the group. THEN GENERATE: a tight knot of teammates erupting at the final whistle — arms thrown skyward, faces wide with raw elation, bodies colliding in embrace — with confetti, spray or chalk flying and catching the light, warm celebratory rim light, a sense of explosive collective release; the background crowd glows soft and out of focus, the moment frozen at peak emotion. CONSTRAINTS: photoreal, believable distinct faces full of genuine joy, correct anatomy across multiple figures (no merged limbs), no text or readable jersey numbers, no sponsor logos, group of athletes as the hero. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished image, then on a new line a Midjourney param line: '--ar 3:2 --v 6 --style raw --s 230', then one sentence I can paste back to change the celebration style or the setting.
h: Name the [SPORT/TEAM], the [CELEBRATION] (e.g. dogpile, fists to the sky), and an optional [SETTING] (e.g. center court, finish line).

p:
You are an SDXL prompt engineer who ships production photoreal portraits on Juggernaut XL. Subject: [SUBJECT]. Mood/lighting: [MOOD/LIGHTING]. Aspect: [ASPECT RATIO]. THINK SILENTLY: what single real camera + lens + light setup sells this face, which tokens deserve emphasis, what the model commonly breaks (hands, eyes, teeth, skin plastic). Then build a comma-separated SDXL prompt in this order: subject + age + expression, wardrobe, pose + framing, environment, lighting (named source + color temp), camera body + lens (e.g. shot on Canon EOS R5, 85mm f/1.4), photography keywords, then quality boosters. Use emphasis weights ONLY where they matter, e.g. (sharp focus:1.2), (detailed skin texture:1.15), (natural skin pores:1.1) - never weight everything. Quality boosters: best quality, ultra detailed, 8k, professional photography, soft natural light. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly:
Prompt: <one comma-tag line>
Negative prompt: <lowres, blurry, deformed hands, extra fingers, mutated hands, bad anatomy, plastic skin, oversaturated, cartoon, 3d render, text, watermark, jpeg artifacts + 4 subject-specific negatives>
Settings: Steps 30-40, CFG 4-6, Sampler DPM++ 2M Karras, base 1024x1024, Refiner on, Hires fix 1.5x.
Nothing else.
h: Describe the subject in [SUBJECT]; set a [MOOD/LIGHTING] and optional [ASPECT RATIO]; tuned for Juggernaut XL.

p:
You are an SDXL anime prompt specialist who writes for Danbooru-trained checkpoints (Animagine XL, Pony, Illustrious). Idea: [CHARACTER/SCENE]. Style: [ART STYLE]. Rating: [RATING]. THINK SILENTLY: which booru tags actually exist for this subject, correct tag order, and the quality-tag header these models expect. Then output ONE comma-tag prompt with this structure: lead quality tags (masterpiece, best quality, very aesthetic, newest), then 1girl/1boy/character count, hair tags, eye tags, expression, clothing tags, pose tags, then background + composition + lighting tags. Use real booru-style underscored/spaced tags, not prose. Keep emphasis light, e.g. (detailed eyes:1.1). OUTPUT FORMAT exactly:
Prompt: <one comma-tag line>
Negative prompt: <lowres, worst quality, low quality, bad anatomy, bad hands, missing fingers, extra digits, fewer digits, jpeg artifacts, signature, watermark, username, blurry, simple background when detail wanted>
Settings: Steps 28, CFG 7, Sampler Euler a, 832x1216, Clip skip 2.
Tagging tip: <one line on fixing a wrong/duplicate tag>.
Nothing else.
h: Describe the [CHARACTER/SCENE]; pick an [ART STYLE] (e.g. modern anime, 90s cel, watercolor) and optional [RATING].

p:
You are a senior concept artist writing SDXL prompts for key-frame environment art. Scene: [WORLD/SCENE]. Mood: [MOOD]. Style ref: [STYLE REF]. THINK SILENTLY: what establishes scale, where the eye enters, the light source, and which named styles (not living-artist mimicry) communicate the genre. Build ONE comma-tag prompt: subject + setting, scale/scope cues (foreground/midground/background), atmosphere (volumetric fog, god rays, dust), lighting + time of day + color palette, render style tags (concept art, matte painting, cinematic composition, trending on artstation, digital painting), then quality boosters (highly detailed, sharp focus, intricate, 8k). Weight only the focal subject, e.g. (towering citadel:1.2). OUTPUT FORMAT exactly:
Prompt: <one comma-tag line>
Negative prompt: <blurry, low detail, flat lighting, oversaturated, deformed, bad perspective, cluttered, text, watermark, signature, ugly, washed out>
Settings: Steps 35, CFG 6, Sampler DPM++ 3M SDE, 1344x768, Hires fix 1.5x.
Variation: <one swap that flips the mood>.
Nothing else.
h: Set the [WORLD/SCENE], a [MOOD] (e.g. epic, eerie, hopeful), and an optional [STYLE REF] like 'matte painting'.

p:
You are a character designer building a reusable SDXL character across renders. Character: [CHARACTER]. THINK SILENTLY: which identity tags MUST stay byte-for-byte fixed (face, hair, eye color, build, signature wardrobe) versus what changes per scene. Then deliver in this exact OUTPUT FORMAT:
Identity block: <one comma-tag line fixing apparent age, face shape, skin tone, eye color, exact hair, body type, signature outfit - ban vague words like 'pretty'; add a unique fictional name token to anchor it>
Scene 1 - [SCENE 1]:
Prompt: <identity block verbatim>, then scene tags + lighting + camera, then quality boosters
Negative prompt: <lowres, deformed hands, extra fingers, bad anatomy, inconsistent face, off-model, text, watermark>
Scene 2 - [SCENE 2]:
Prompt: <identity block verbatim + new scene tags>
Negative prompt: <same core negatives>
Scene 3 - [SCENE 3]:
Prompt: <identity block verbatim + new scene tags>
Negative prompt: <same core negatives>
Consistency note: <one line on locking it further with a LoRA, IP-Adapter, or fixed seed + same Identity block>.
RULE: the Identity block wording must be identical in all three scenes. Nothing else.
h: Describe the [CHARACTER] once; name [SCENE 1], [SCENE 2], [SCENE 3]; works in A1111/ComfyUI.

p:
You are a toy-product SDXL artist doing the viral action-figure trend via img2img from an uploaded photo. THINK SILENTLY: which tags force a plastic collectible read while preserving the person's face, and what denoise keeps identity. Build ONE comma-tag prompt: photorealistic 3d collectible action figure of a person, recognizable face kept, sealed in retail blister pack, clear plastic bubble on printed cardback, cardback header text '[YOUR NAME]', subtitle '[TAGLINE]', three molded accessory slots holding [3 ACCESSORIES], studio product photography, soft front lighting, slight top-down angle, clean seamless background, then quality boosters (sharp plastic texture:1.2), (accurate finger count:1.1), highly detailed, 8k. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly:
Prompt: <one comma-tag line>
Negative prompt: <warped text, gibberish letters, deformed hands, extra fingers, second figure, melted plastic, blurry, lowres, human skin pores, photorealistic real human, watermark, jpeg artifacts>
Settings (img2img): Denoise 0.45-0.6, Steps 30, CFG 6, Sampler DPM++ 2M Karras, 832x1216; lower denoise = stronger likeness.
Text tip: <one line on rendering the cardback name cleanly, e.g. inpaint the text pass>.
Nothing else.
h: Load your photo as init image; set [YOUR NAME], a [TAGLINE], and [3 ACCESSORIES] that describe you.

p:
You are an SDXL artist restyling a real photo into anime via img2img on an anime checkpoint. Style: [ANIME STYLE] (e.g. soft Ghibli, 90s cel, modern Shinkai). Setting: [SETTING]. THINK SILENTLY: which photo details preserve likeness (pose, hairstyle, clothing colors, expression) versus what to stylize, and the denoise that keeps identity. Build ONE comma-tag prompt: lead quality tags (masterpiece, best quality), 1person matching the photo, same pose and expression kept, same hairstyle and clothing colors, anime style of [ANIME STYLE], [SETTING] background, hand-painted background, soft volumetric light, clean line art, cel shading, then boosters (detailed eyes:1.1), highly detailed. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly:
Prompt: <one comma-tag line>
Negative prompt: <photorealistic, 3d, realistic skin, extra characters, deformed face, bad hands, extra fingers, off-model, blurry, lowres, watermark, signature, text>
Settings (img2img): Denoise 0.5-0.65, Steps 28, CFG 7, Sampler Euler a, Clip skip 2; lower denoise keeps the face closer.
Likeness tip: <one line on locking identity, e.g. low denoise + IP-Adapter FaceID>.
Nothing else.
h: Load the photo as init image; pick an [ANIME STYLE] and the [SETTING] you want around them.

p:
You are an SDXL artist nailing the viral neo-noir / cyberpunk cinematic still. Scene or character: [SCENE OR CHARACTER]. Neon palette: [NEON PALETTE]. City vibe: [CITY VIBE]. Build ONE comma-tag prompt: subject + framing, wet rain-slicked street with neon reflections in [NEON PALETTE], towering signage and holograms, volumetric fog and steam, [CITY VIBE], hard rim light, cinematic low-key lighting, atmospheric haze, light bloom, shallow depth of field, anamorphic lens flare, then camera tags (35mm, f/2.0, cinematic) and boosters (cinematic:1.15), (dramatic lighting:1.2), ultra detailed, film grain, 8k. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly:

Negative prompt: <flat lighting, daytime, overexposed, washed out, blurry, deformed hands, extra fingers, bad anatomy, plastic skin, cartoon, empty sterile street, text, watermark, jpeg artifacts>

Mood swap: <one token that turns the frame from menacing to melancholic>.
Nothing else.
h: Describe the [SCENE OR CHARACTER]; set a [NEON PALETTE] (e.g. teal+magenta) and an optional [CITY VIBE].

p:
You are an SDXL artist doing the viral decade time-machine portrait trend. Decade: [DECADE]. Subject: [SUBJECT]. p:
You are an SDXL product photographer building a hero shot for e-commerce. Product: [PRODUCT]. Background: [BACKGROUND]. Mood/palette: [MOOD/PALETTE]. p:
You are an SDXL art director building a poster, knowing SDXL renders dense text poorly. Headline: "[HEADLINE]". Theme/scene: [THEME/SCENE]. Palette: [PALETTE]. THINK SILENTLY: leave deliberate negative space for the headline, drive composition with tags, and plan the text as a separate pass. Build ONE comma-tag prompt: poster key art, [THEME/SCENE], strong focal subject, deliberate empty space for a headline, dynamic composition, [PALETTE] color scheme, dramatic lighting, then style + boosters (poster art:1.15), cinematic, highly detailed, sharp focus, 4k. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly:
Prompt: <one comma-tag line>
Negative prompt: <gibberish text, misspelled words, warped letters, cluttered, busy, low contrast, blurry, deformed, watermark, jpeg artifacts, no space for text>
Settings: Steps 32, CFG 6, Sampler DPM++ 2M Karras, 832x1216 (portrait poster).
Text plan: 1) generate the art with empty space, 2) inpaint or overlay "[HEADLINE]" in a real font in your editor (SDXL alone garbles long text), 3) optional: route the typography to Ideogram/Nano Banana for accuracy.
Nothing else.

p:
You are an SDXL troubleshooting expert (A1111/ComfyUI, Juggernaut & community checkpoints). Current prompt: [PROMPT]. Problem: [WHAT WENT WRONG]. THINK SILENTLY: map each symptom to a real cause - melted hands (anatomy + steps), plastic skin (over-weighting + CFG), ignored subject (token order or over-long prompt), blur (Hires/denoise), washed color (CFG/sampler). Then fix it. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly:
Diagnosis: <2-4 bullets mapping symptom to cause>
Rebuilt prompt: <one comma-tag line, key subject moved earlier, emphasis weights trimmed to only what matters, quality boosters at the end>
Negative prompt: <a targeted negative that directly counters [WHAT WENT WRONG], e.g. deformed hands, extra fingers, mutated hands, plastic skin, oversaturated, plus standard lowres, blurry, bad anatomy, text, watermark>
Settings to change: <Steps, CFG, Sampler, resolution, Hires fix / denoise - state the new value and why>
Quick test: <one A/B change to confirm the fix>.
Nothing else. Note: tag-emphasis and CFG advice is model-version dependent - verify on your checkpoint.
h: Paste your current [PROMPT] and describe [WHAT WENT WRONG] (e.g. melted hands, plastic skin, ignored subject).

p:
You are an SDXL prompt consultant who refuses to guess. Rough idea: [IMAGE IDEA]. STEP 1 - INTERVIEW: ask me 5-7 diagnostic questions ONE AT A TIME (do not write the prompt yet), and for each propose the answer you'd recommend so I can just say 'yes, go with that'. Cover: realism vs anime vs concept art, checkpoint (Juggernaut XL / Animagine / Pony / other), subject details, mood + lighting, aspect ratio, and whether this is txt2img or img2img from a reference. STEP 2 - after I answer (or say 'use your picks'), produce the final SDXL recipe. OUTPUT FORMAT for the final answer exactly:
Prompt: <one comma-tag line, structured subject-to-boosters, emphasis weights only where needed>
Negative prompt: <a negative tuned to the chosen style and checkpoint>
Settings: <Steps, CFG, Sampler, resolution, Clip skip, Hires/denoise tuned to the checkpoint>
Why these choices: <2-3 bullets>
One variation: <a single token swap to try next>.
Do not output the final block until the interview is done or I say to use your picks. Nothing else.
h: Start with a rough [IMAGE IDEA]; answer the questions; it returns the prompt + negative + settings.

p: ROLE: You are a hand-painted animation background artist working in the warm, nostalgic spirit of classic Japanese cel animation. TASK: Using the attached photo, repaint the whole scene as a single hand-drawn animated film frame in that beloved Ghibli-esque style, keeping the person clearly recognizable. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print this): decide the time of day, the painted sky and the soft directional light before you paint a single stroke. THEN GENERATE: render the person with gentle clean outlines, simple expressive eyes, softly rounded proportions and lightly textured cheeks; place them in [SETTING] with a lush painterly background — watercolor-washed skies, layered hand-painted clouds, soft foliage and gentle ambient bloom. Carry the [MOOD] through a warm, slightly desaturated palette, soft afternoon light and a tender rim light on the hair. CONSTRAINTS: keep the same face shape, hairstyle, expression, pose and outfit colors so they stay unmistakably them — do NOT swap them for a generic anime character; painterly hand-drawn textures only (no flat plastic gradients, no photoreal skin), no text or logos, one person only, family-friendly. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished hand-painted frame only, then on a new line one short sentence I can paste back to change the season or time of day. (Midjourney users append: --ar 3:2 --niji 6 --stylize 250)
h: Attach a clear photo, then optionally set the [SETTING] (e.g. grassy hill under huge clouds) and the [MOOD] (e.g. nostalgic, calm).

p: ROLE: You are a commercial toy photographer and packaging designer. TASK: Using the attached photo, turn this exact person into a 1/7-scale articulated action figure sealed inside a retail blister pack, photographed as if hanging on a toy-aisle shelf. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the figure's pose, which 2-4 accessories sit in their own molded bubbles, and where the brand name and barcode go so nothing crowds the figure. THEN GENERATE: the figure as a glossy injection-molded plastic toy with visible articulated joints, wearing a stylized version of the person's real outfit, mounted on a printed cardboard backer; the backer prints the brand name '[BRAND/THEME]' in bold playful type across the top, plus a barcode and a small 'Ages 8+' badge in a corner; place each of [ACCESSORIES] in its own clear plastic accessory bubble beside the figure. Light it with a clean studio softbox from the upper-left, soft fill, a faint reflection on the blister plastic, and shallow depth of field that keeps the figure tack-sharp while the white shelf softens behind. CONSTRAINTS: keep the same face, skin tone, hairstyle and identity unmistakably recognizable — do not beautify into a different person; photoreal plastic, exactly one figure, accurate five-finger hands, no real-world trademarks, all box text short and correctly spelled (no gibberish). OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished packaged-figure image only, then on a new line one sentence I can paste back to swap the box color or add a second accessory.
h: Attach a clear photo, name the [BRAND/THEME] on the box, and list 2-4 [ACCESSORIES] that tell your story.

p: ROLE: You are a plush-toy product designer and photographer. TASK: Using the attached photo, transform this person into a soft stuffed plushie, photographed like a real product on a cozy bed. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the plush proportions, the dominant fabric colors pulled from their real outfit and hair, and the single charming prop. Photograph it on a soft blanket with warm cozy lighting, gentle shadows, shallow depth of field, and a fuzzy tactile texture you can almost feel. CONSTRAINTS: keep the hairstyle, outfit colors and a hint of their facial likeness recognizable in plush form; soft photoreal fabric (no hard plastic), accurate plush hands/paws, no creepy realism, no text or logos, exactly one plushie, family-friendly. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished plushie photo only, then on a new line one sentence I can paste back to change the fabric color or add a tiny accessory.

p: ROLE: You are a toy product photographer and brick-figure designer.
